revision.comment: 78 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:薦神社 up to rev 101324642
revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 薦神社 |画像 = [[File:Komo.jpg|280px]]
神門 |所在地 = [[大分県]][[中津市]]大字大貞209 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|33|34|2.2|N|131|13|5.1|E|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = [[応神天皇]]
[[比売大神]]
[[息長帯比売命]] |社格 = 旧[[県社]] |創建 = [[承和 (日本)|承和]]年間（9世紀前葉） |本殿 = [[流造]] |別名 = 大貞八幡宮 |札所等 = |例祭 = 4月21日 |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Oita|lat_deg=33|lat_min=34|lat_sec=2.2|lon_deg=131|lon_min=13|lon_sec=5.1|label=薦神社|position=top|width=210|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=210|coord={{coord|33|34|2.2|N|131|13|5.1|E}}}} }} '''薦神社'''（こもじんじゃ）は、[[大分県]][[中津市]]に所在する[[八幡宮]]で、別名'''大貞八幡宮'''（おおさだはちまんぐう）とも称される。境内の三角池（みすみいけ：古くは「御澄池」と記すこともある）を内宮、神殿を外宮とする。全国[[八幡宮]]の総本宮である[[宇佐神宮]]の祖宮といわれ、[[承和 (日本)|承和]]年間（834-848年）の創建と伝える古社である。 == 祭神 == * 本殿 - [[応神天皇]]（八幡大神）、[[比売神|比咩大神]]（宗像三女神）、[[神功皇后|息長帯比売命]]（神功皇后） * 八坂社（祇園社） - [[スサノオ|素盞嗚尊]]（すさのおのみこと） * 若宮社 - 応神天皇皇子 比咩大神とは[[宗像三女神]]のことで、沖津宮（[[沖ノ島]]）の[[タキリビメ|田心比売命]]（たごりひめのみこと）、中津宮（[[福岡県]][[大島 (福岡県)|大島]]）の[[タギツヒメ|瑞津比売命]]（たぎつひめのみこと）、辺津宮（へつみや）（同県[[宗像市]]田島）の[[イチキシマヒメ|市杵島比売命]]（いちきしまひめのみこと）を示す。この三女神は『[[日本書紀]]』巻第1神代上第6段一書第3の条によると、「則ち日神の生れまする三の女神を以ては、葦原中国（あしはらのなかつくに）の宇佐島に降り居さしむ。今、海の北の道の中に在す」とあり、最初は宇佐の地に降り立ったことを示している。 == 社紋 == 一つ巴。元来、[[巴紋]]は水に由来する紋で、宝器の[[勾玉]]、魂、命に関連する。宇佐神宮の三つ巴に対して、一つ巴であることから、宇佐の祖宮の一つと考えられている薦神社は、本来は宗像三比売大神ではなく、別の、あるいはその中の一人の比売大神（たとえば中津宮の湍津姫神）を祀っていたのではないかという説もある。 == 歴史 == [[養老]]4年（[[720年]]）、[[大隅国|大隅]]・[[日向国|日向]]の[[隼人の反乱]]（大隅国府襲撃）で[[大伴旅人]]が率いる[[大和朝廷]]軍および宇佐神宮の[[辛島波豆米]]（からしまのはづめ）率いる宇佐「神軍」が、薦神社の三角池に自生する[[マコモ|真薦]]を刈って作った枕形の御験、薦枕（こもまくら）を[[神体]]に、[[神輿]]を奉じて日向まで[[行幸]]し、乱を鎮めたと言われる。{{要出典範囲|date=2011年8月|この薦刈神事は現在6年ごとに行われる[[宇佐神宮行幸会]]の中で辛嶋一族が当時より行い伝えている}}。 {{要出典範囲|date=2024年6月|大貞の「貞」の字には占いの意味があると言われ、[[道教]]との関連性も指摘されている}}。 薦神社と三角池は、「三角池と薦神社」として[[昭和]]51年（[[1976年]]）3月30日に大分県指定史跡、「三角池の水生・湿地植物群落」が大分県指定天然記念物に指定されており、神門は[[元和 (日本)|元和]]8年（[[1622年]]）に[[細川忠興]]の造営で、国の[[重要文化財]]に指定されている。社殿は江戸時代末期に建造[https://www.city-nakatsu.jp/kankodocs/2013080200152/ 中津市観光公式サイト]。 == 祭祀 == * 2月11日 - 鎮疫祭、御心経会（鬼やらい行事） * 4月21日 - 例祭 * 9月第3土･日曜日 - 仲秋祭（御神幸祭） == 交通 == * [[九州旅客鉄道|JR九州]][[小倉駅 (福岡県)|小倉駅]]より、[[日豊本線]]の特急[[ソニック (列車)|ソニック]]で約30分で[[中津駅 (大分県)|中津駅]]下車。駅から車で約15分。 * 路線バス利用の場合は、中津駅南口より[[大交北部バス]]田中行きまたは少年学院官舎前行きに乗車、薦神社前下車。所要約20分。 == 参考文献 == *『写真集 薦神社 神門編』、薦文化研究所、平成11年 *谷川健一編『日本の神々 - 神社と聖地』第1巻九州、白水社、1984ISBN 4-560-02211-9 *『大分県の文化財』、大分県教育委員会、平成3年 == 関連項目 == *[[秦氏]] *[[大悟法久志]] == 脚注 == == 外部リンク == * [http://www.komojinja.jp/ 薦神社]（神社公式） {{神道 横}} {{Normdaten}} {{DEFAULTSORT:こもしんしや}} [[Category:大分県の神社]] [[Category:八幡宮]] [[Category:大分県の旧県社]] [[Category:神功皇后を祭神とした神社]] [[Category:重要文化財 (建造物)]] [[Category:大分県の重要文化財]] [[Category:大分県指定史跡]] [[Category:大分県指定天然記念物]] [[Category:江戸時代の建築]] [[Category:17世紀の建築物]] [[Category:中津市の建築物]] [[Category:中津市の歴史]]

[[比売大神]]
[[息長帯比売命]] |社格 = 旧[[県社]] |創建 = [[承和 (日本)|承和]]年間（9世紀前葉） |本殿 = [[流造]] |別名 = 大貞八幡宮 |札所等 = |例祭 = 4月21日 |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Oita|lat_deg=33|lat_min=34|lat_sec=2.2|lon_deg=131|lon_min=13|lon_sec=5.1|label=薦神社|position=top|width=210|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=210|coord={{coord|33|34|2.2|N|131|13|5.1|E}}}} }} '''薦神社'''（こもじんじゃ）は、[[大分県]][[中津市]]に所在する[[八幡宮]]で、別名'''大貞八幡宮'''（おおさだはちまんぐう）とも称される。境内の三角池（みすみいけ：古くは「御澄池」と記すこともある）を内宮、神殿を外宮とする。全国[[八幡宮]]の総本宮である[[宇佐神宮]]の祖宮といわれ、[[承和 (日本)|承和]]年間（834-848年）の創建と伝える古社である。 == 祭神 == * 本殿 - [[応神天皇]]（八幡大神）、[[比売神|比咩大神]]（宗像三女神）、[[神功皇后|息長帯比売命]]（神功皇后） * 八坂社（祇園社） - [[スサノオ|素盞嗚尊]]（すさのおのみこと） * 若宮社 - 応神天皇皇子 比咩大神とは[[宗像三女神]]のことで、沖津宮（[[沖ノ島]]）の[[タキリビメ|田心比売命]]（たごりひめのみこと）、中津宮（[[福岡県]][[大島 (福岡県)|大島]]）の[[タギツヒメ|瑞津比売命]]（たぎつひめのみこと）、辺津宮（へつみや）（同県[[宗像市]]田島）の[[イチキシマヒメ|市杵島比売命]]（いちきしまひめのみこと）を示す。この三女神は『[[日本書紀]]』巻第1神代上第6段一書第3の条によると、「則ち日神の生れまする三の女神を以ては、葦原中国（あしはらのなかつくに）の宇佐島に降り居さしむ。今、海の北の道の中に在す」とあり、最初は宇佐の地に降り立ったことを示している。 == 社紋 == 一つ巴。元来、[[巴紋]]は水に由来する紋で、宝器の[[勾玉]]、魂、命に関連する。宇佐神宮の三つ巴に対して、一つ巴であることから、宇佐の祖宮の一つと考えられている薦神社は、本来は宗像三比売大神ではなく、別の、あるいはその中の一人の比売大神（たとえば中津宮の湍津姫神）を祀っていたのではないかという説もある。 == 歴史 == [[養老]]4年（[[720年]]）、[[大隅国|大隅]]・[[日向国|日向]]の[[隼人の反乱]]（大隅国府襲撃）で[[大伴旅人]]が率いる[[大和朝廷]]軍および宇佐神宮の[[辛島波豆米]]（からしまのはづめ）率いる宇佐「神軍」が、薦神社の三角池に自生する[[マコモ|真薦]]を刈って作った枕形の御験、薦枕（こもまくら）を[[神体]]に、[[神輿]]を奉じて日向まで[[行幸]]し、乱を鎮めたと言われる。{{要出典範囲|date=2011年8月|この薦刈神事は現在6年ごとに行われる[[宇佐神宮行幸会]]の中で辛嶋一族が当時より行い伝えている}}。 {{要出典範囲|date=2024年6月|大貞の「貞」の字には占いの意味があると言われ、[[道教]]との関連性も指摘されている}}。 薦神社と三角池は、「三角池と薦神社」として[[昭和]]51年（[[1976年]]）3月30日に大分県指定史跡、「三角池の水生・湿地植物群落」が大分県指定天然記念物に指定されており、神門は[[元和 (日本)|元和]]8年（[[1622年]]）に[[細川忠興]]の造営で、国の[[重要文化財]]に指定されている。社殿は江戸時代末期に建造[https://www.city-nakatsu.jp/kankodocs/2013080200152/ 中津市観光公式サイト]。 == 祭祀 == * 2月11日 - 鎮疫祭、御心経会（鬼やらい行事） * 4月21日 - 例祭 * 9月第3土･日曜日 - 仲秋祭（御神幸祭） == 交通 == * [[九州旅客鉄道|JR九州]][[小倉駅 (福岡県)|小倉駅]]より、[[日豊本線]]の特急[[ソニック (列車)|ソニック]]で約30分で[[中津駅 (大分県)|中津駅]]下車。駅から車で約15分。 * 路線バス利用の場合は、中津駅南口より[[大交北部バス]]田中行きまたは少年学院官舎前行きに乗車、薦神社前下車。所要約20分。 == 参考文献 == *『写真集 薦神社 神門編』、薦文化研究所、平成11年 *谷川健一編『日本の神々 - 神社と聖地』第1巻九州、白水社、1984ISBN 4-560-02211-9 *『大分県の文化財』、大分県教育委員会、平成3年 == 関連項目 == *[[秦氏]] *[[大悟法久志]] == 脚注 == == 外部リンク == * [http://www.komojinja.jp/ 薦神社]（神社公式） {{神道 横}} {{Normdaten}} {{DEFAULTSORT:こもしんしや}} [[Category:大分県の神社]] [[Category:八幡宮]] [[Category:大分県の旧県社]] [[Category:神功皇后を祭神とした神社]] [[Category:重要文化財 (建造物)]] [[Category:大分県の重要文化財]] [[Category:大分県指定史跡]] [[Category:大分県指定天然記念物]] [[Category:江戸時代の建築]] [[Category:17世紀の建築物]] [[Category:中津市の建築物]] [[Category:中津市の歴史]]

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Komo Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Komo.jpg|280px|Main Gate]]
Shinmon (Sacred Gate) | A11ress = {{ill|Ōita Prefecture|ja|大分県}}, {{ill|Nakatsu, Ōita|ja|中津市}}, Ōaza Osada 209 | coordinates = {{coord|33|34|2.2|N|131|13|5.1|E|region:JP-44_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | Deities = {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}}
{{ill|Hime-no-Kami|ja|比売大神|lt=Hime-no-Ōkami|1=Himegami|3=比売神}}
{{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|息長帯比売命}} | Status = Former [[Prefectural Shrine]] (県社) | Established = [[Shōwa era (Japan)|Shōwa era]] (9th century) | Main Hall = {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} | Alternate name = Ōsada Hachiman Shrine (大貞八幡宮) | Festivals = April 21 (festival) | Map = {{location map|Japan Oita|label=Komo Shrine|lat_deg=33|lat_min=34|lat_sec=2.2|lon_deg=131|lon_min=13|lon_sec=5.1|position=top|width=210|float=center|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=210|coord={{coord|33|34|2.2|N|131|13|5.1|E}}}} }} '''Komo Shrine''' (薦神社) is a {{ill|Hachiman Shrine|ja|八幡宮}} located in {{ill|Nakatsu, Ōita|ja|中津市}}, {{ill|Ōita Prefecture|ja|大分県}}. It is also referred to as the Ōsada Hachiman Shrine (大貞八幡宮). The shrine features a sacred triangular pond, Misumi Pond (三角池, also historically written as 御澄池), representing the inner shrine, while the sacred hall is considered the outer shrine. This ancient shrine is said to be the progenitor of {{ill|Usa Shrine|ja|宇佐神宮}}, the primary shrine of all Hachiman shrines in Japan, with origins dating to the {{ill|Jōwa (Heian period|lt=Jōwa era|ja|承和}} (834–848). For this reason it is often called "Moto-Usa" == Enshrined Deities == * '''Main Hall''': ''' {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}} (Hachiman Ōkami) ''' {{ill|Hime-no-Kami|ja|比咩大神}} (The Three Go11esses of Munakata) ''' {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|息長帯比売命}} * '''Yasaka Shrine (Gion Shrine)''': ''' {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞嗚尊}} (Susanoo-no-Mikoto) * '''Wakamiya Shrine''': ''' Offspring of Emperor Ōjin Hime-no-Kami refers to the {{ill|Three Go11esses of Munakata|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=Three Munakata Go11esses}}, represented by: * '''Tagorihime-no-Mikoto''' (沖津宮 at {{ill|Okinoshima|ja|沖ノ島|en}}) * '''Tatsutsuhime-no-Mikoto''' (中津宮 at {{ill|Ōshima (Fukuoka)|ja|大島_(福岡県)|fa|اوشیما (استان فوکوئوکا)|ceb|Ō_Shima_(pulo_sa_Hapon,_Fukuoka-ken)}}) * '''Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto''' (辺津宮 at {{ill|Munakata Taisha|ja|宗像市田島}}). According to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, Volume 1, Kami Age, Chapter 6, Variation 3, the Three Go11esses were said to have descended at Usa Island in Ashihara-no-Nakatsukuni, marking their connection to this sacred site. == Shrine Crest == The shrine uses the "Single Tomoe" crest (一つ巴). Unlike the "Triple Tomoe" of Usa Shrine, the Single Tomoe indicates Komo Shrine's status as a progenitor shrine. Some scholars suggest that the shrine originally enshrined one of the Three Go11esses, possibly Tatsutsuhime-no-Mikoto, rather than all three. == History == In {{ill|Yōrō|ja|養老}} 4 (720), during the {{ill|Hayato Rebellion|ja|隼人の反乱}}, the forces led by {{ill|Ōtomo no Tabito|ja|大伴旅人}} and Usa's "Divine Army," under Karashima Haduome, used sacred reed mats (薦枕, Komomakura) from Misumi Pond as a divine artifact to quell the rebellion. The Komokari Festival commemorates this event and is still performed every six years as part of the {{ill|Usa Shrine Procession|ja|宇佐神宮行幸会}} by the Karashima clan. Komo Shrine and Misumi Pond were designated as: * '''Ōita Prefectural Historic Site''' (March 30, 1976) * '''Ōita Prefectural Natural Monument''' for its aquatic and wetland plant communities. The Shinmon Gate, constructed by {{ill|Hosokawa Tadaoki|ja|細川忠興}} in {{ill|Genna|ja|元和}} 8 (1622), is a {{ill|National Treasure of Japan|ja|重要文化財|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)|2=ja|3=重要文化財|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|4=ko|5=일본의_중요문화재}}. The shrine buildings date to the late Edo period. == Annual Rituals == * '''February 11''': Chinyoku Festival and Goshinkō-e (Demon Banishing Ceremony) * '''April 21''': festival * '''Third Saturday and Sunday in September''': Nakashū Festival (Sacred Procession) == Access == * From {{ill|Kokura Station|ja|小倉駅}}: Take the {{ill|Sonic (train)|ja|ソニック}}, a limited express train on the {{ill|Nippō Main Line|ja|日豊本線}}, to {{ill|Nakatsu Station|ja|中津駅}} (approximately 30 minutes). A 15-minute drive from the station. * By Bus: From Nakatsu Station South Exit, take a {{ill|Daiwa Kotsu|ja|大交北部バス}} bound for Tanaka or Shōnen Gakuin-Kansha-mae and alight at "Komo Shrine" (approximately 20 minutes). * By Car: Approximately 15 minutes from the Nakatsu IC on the {{ill|Nippō Expressway|ja|日豊自動車道}}. == References == * ''Photographic Collection: Komo Shrine, Shinmon Volume,'' Komo Culture Research Institute, 1999 * Kenichi Tanigawa (Ed.), ''Japanese Gods and Sacred Sites, Volume 1: Kyūshū,'' Hakusuisha, 1984, ISBN 4-560-02211-9 * ''Cultural Properties of Ōita Prefecture,'' Ōita Prefectural Board of Education, 1991 == See Also == * {{ill|Hata clan|ja|秦氏}} * {{ill|Daigo Hisa|ja|大悟法久志}} == External Links == * [http://www.komojinja.jp/ Official Website of Komo Shrine] {{Shinto navbox}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Komo Shrine (Oita)]] [[Category:Shrines in Ōita Prefecture]] [[Category:Hachiman Shrines]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines]] [[Category:Shrines dedicated to Empress Jingū]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Historic Sites in Ōita Prefecture]] [[Category:Natural Monuments in Ōita Prefecture]] [[Category:Edo Period architecture]] [[Category:17th-century architecture]] [[Category:Nakatsu, Ōita]] [[Category:Komo Shrine]] }} [[sn:Komo Shrine (Oita)]] [[ja:薦神社]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Komo Shrine (Oita)}} {{translated page|ja|薦神社|version=101324642|comment=Imported full ja history}}

The shrine features a sacred triangular pond, Misumi Pond (三角池, also historically written as 御澄池), representing the inner shrine, while the sacred hall is considered the outer shrine. This ancient shrine is said to be the progenitor of {{ill|Usa Shrine|ja|宇佐神宮|12=draft}}, the primary shrine of all Hachiman shrines in Japan, with origins dating to the {{ill|Jōwa (Heian period|lt=Jōwa era|ja|承和|12=draft}} (834–848). For this reason it is often called "Moto-Usa" == Enshrined Deities == * '''Main Hall''': ''' {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇|12=draft}} (Hachiman Ōkami) ''' {{ill|Hime-no-Kami|ja|比咩大神|12=draft}} (The Three Go11esses of Munakata) ''' {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|息長帯比売命|12=draft}} * '''Yasaka Shrine (Gion Shrine)''': ''' {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞嗚尊|12=draft}} (Susanoo-no-Mikoto) * '''Wakamiya Shrine''': ''' Offspring of Emperor Ōjin Hime-no-Kami refers to the {{ill|Three Go11esses of Munakata|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=Three Munakata Go11esses|12=draft}}, represented by: * '''Tagorihime-no-Mikoto''' (沖津宮 at {{ill|Okinoshima|ja|沖ノ島|en|12=draft}}) * '''Tatsutsuhime-no-Mikoto''' (中津宮 at {{ill|Ōshima (Fukuoka)|ja|大島_(福岡県)|fa|اوشیما (استان فوکوئوکا)|ceb|Ō_Shima_(pulo_sa_Hapon,_Fukuoka-ken)|12=draft}}) * '''Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto''' (辺津宮 at {{ill|Munakata Taisha|ja|宗像市田島|12=draft}}). According to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|12=draft}}, Volume 1, Kami Age, Chapter 6, Variation 3, the Three Go11esses were said to have descended at Usa Island in Ashihara-no-Nakatsukuni, marking their connection to this sacred site. == Shrine Crest == The shrine uses the "Single Tomoe" crest (一つ巴). Unlike the "Triple Tomoe" of Usa Shrine, the Single Tomoe indicates Komo Shrine's status as a progenitor shrine. Some scholars suggest that the shrine originally enshrined one of the Three Go11esses, possibly Tatsutsuhime-no-Mikoto, rather than all three. == History == In {{ill|Yōrō|ja|養老|12=draft}} 4 (720), during the {{ill|Hayato Rebellion|ja|隼人の反乱|12=draft}}, the forces led by {{ill|Ōtomo no Tabito|ja|大伴旅人|12=draft}} and Usa's "Divine Army," under Karashima Haduome, used sacred reed mats (薦枕, Komomakura) from Misumi Pond as a divine artifact to quell the rebellion. The Komokari Festival commemorates this event and is still performed every six years as part of the {{ill|Usa Shrine Procession|ja|宇佐神宮行幸会|12=draft}} by the Karashima clan. Komo Shrine and Misumi Pond were designated as: * '''Ōita Prefectural Historic Site''' (March 30, 1976) * '''Ōita Prefectural Natural Monument''' for its aquatic and wetland plant communities. The Shinmon Gate, constructed by {{ill|Hosokawa Tadaoki|ja|細川忠興|12=draft}} in {{ill|Genna|ja|元和|12=draft}} 8 (1622), is a {{ill|National Treasure of Japan|ja|重要文化財|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)|2=ja|3=重要文化財|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|4=ko|5=일본의_중요문화재|12=draft}}. The shrine buildings date to the late Edo period. == Annual Rituals == * '''February 11''': Chinyoku Festival and Goshinkō-e (Demon Banishing Ceremony) * '''April 21''': festival * '''Third Saturday and Sunday in September''': Nakashū Festival (Sacred Procession) == Access == * From {{ill|Kokura Station|ja|小倉駅|12=draft}}: Take the {{ill|Sonic (train)|ja|ソニック|12=draft}}, a limited express train on the {{ill|Nippō Main Line|ja|日豊本線|12=draft}}, to {{ill|Nakatsu Station|ja|中津駅|12=draft}} (approximately 30 minutes). A 15-minute drive from the station. * By Bus: From Nakatsu Station South Exit, take a {{ill|Daiwa Kotsu|ja|大交北部バス|12=draft}} bound for Tanaka or Shōnen Gakuin-Kansha-mae and alight at "Komo Shrine" (approximately 20 minutes). * By Car: Approximately 15 minutes from the Nakatsu IC on the {{ill|Nippō Expressway|ja|日豊自動車道|12=draft}}. == References == * ''Photographic Collection: Komo Shrine, Shinmon Volume,'' Komo Culture Research Institute, 1999 * Kenichi Tanigawa (Ed.), ''Japanese Gods and Sacred Sites, Volume 1: Kyūshū,'' Hakusuisha, 1984, ISBN 4-560-02211-9 * ''Cultural Properties of Ōita Prefecture,'' Ōita Prefectural Board of Education, 1991 == See Also == * {{ill|Hata clan|ja|秦氏|12=draft}} * {{ill|Daigo Hisa|ja|大悟法久志|12=draft}} == External Links == * [http://www.komojinja.jp/ Official Website of Komo Shrine] {{Shinto navbox}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| }} {{translated page|ja|薦神社|version=101324642|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Komo Shrine (Oita)]] [[Category:Shrines in Ōita Prefecture]] [[Category:Hachiman Shrines]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines]] [[Category:Shrines dedicated to Empress Jingū]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Historic Sites in Ōita Prefecture]] [[Category:Natural Monuments in Ōita Prefecture]] [[Category:Edo Period architecture]] [[Category:17th-century architecture]] [[Category:Nakatsu, Ōita]] [[Category:Komo Shrine]] }} [[sn:Komo Shrine (Oita)]] [[ja:薦神社]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Komo Shrine (Oita)}}

Some scholars suggest that the shrine originally enshrined one of the Three Go11esses, possibly Tatsutsuhime-no-Mikoto, rather than all three. == History == In {{ill|Yōrō|ja|養老|qq=draft}} 4 (720), during the {{ill|Hayato Rebellion|ja|隼人の反乱|qq=draft}}, the forces led by {{ill|Ōtomo no Tabito|ja|大伴旅人|qq=draft}} and Usa's "Divine Army," under Karashima Haduome, used sacred reed mats (薦枕, Komomakura) from Misumi Pond as a divine artifact to quell the rebellion. The Komokari Festival commemorates this event and is still performed every six years as part of the {{ill|Usa Shrine Procession|ja|宇佐神宮行幸会|qq=draft}} by the Karashima clan. Komo Shrine and Misumi Pond were designated as: * '''Ōita Prefectural Historic Site''' (March 30, 1976) * '''Ōita Prefectural Natural Monument''' for its aquatic and wetland plant communities. The Shinmon Gate, constructed by {{ill|Hosokawa Tadaoki|ja|細川忠興|qq=draft}} in {{ill|Genna|ja|元和|qq=draft}} 8 (1622), is a {{ill|National Treasure of Japan|ja|重要文化財|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)|2=ja|3=重要文化財|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|4=ko|5=일본의_중요문화재|qq=draft}}. The shrine buildings date to the late Edo period. == Annual Rituals == * '''February 11''': Chinyoku Festival and Goshinkō-e (Demon Banishing Ceremony) * '''April 21''': festival * '''Third Saturday and Sunday in September''': Nakashū Festival (Sacred Procession) == Access == * From {{ill|Kokura Station|ja|小倉駅|qq=draft}}: Take the {{ill|Sonic (train)|ja|ソニック|qq=draft}}, a limited express train on the {{ill|Nippō Main Line|ja|日豊本線|qq=draft}}, to {{ill|Nakatsu Station|ja|中津駅|qq=draft}} (approximately 30 minutes). A 15-minute drive from the station. * By Bus: From Nakatsu Station South Exit, take a {{ill|Daiwa Kotsu|ja|大交北部バス|qq=draft}} bound for Tanaka or Shōnen Gakuin-Kansha-mae and alight at "Komo Shrine" (approximately 20 minutes). * By Car: Approximately 15 minutes from the Nakatsu IC on the {{ill|Nippō Expressway|ja|日豊自動車道|qq=draft}}. == References == * ''Photographic Collection: Komo Shrine, Shinmon Volume,'' Komo Culture Research Institute, 1999 * Kenichi Tanigawa (Ed.), ''Japanese Gods and Sacred Sites, Volume 1: Kyūshū,'' Hakusuisha, 1984, ISBN 4-560-02211-9 * ''Cultural Properties of Ōita Prefecture,'' Ōita Prefectural Board of Education, 1991 == See Also == * {{ill|Hata clan|ja|秦氏|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Daigo Hisa|ja|大悟法久志|qq=draft}} == External Links == * [http://www.komojinja.jp/ Official Website of Komo Shrine] {{Shinto navbox}} {{translated page|ja|薦神社|version=101324642|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Komo Shrine (Oita)]] [[Category:Shrines in Ōita Prefecture]] [[Category:Hachiman Shrines]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines]] [[Category:Shrines dedicated to Empress Jingū]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Historic Sites in Ōita Prefecture]] [[Category:Natural Monuments in Ōita Prefecture]] [[Category:Edo Period architecture]] [[Category:17th-century architecture]] [[Category:Nakatsu, Ōita]] [[Category:Komo Shrine]] }} [[sn:Komo Shrine (Oita)]] [[ja:薦神社]]

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Komo Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Komo.jpg|280px|Main Gate]]
Shinmon (Sacred Gate) | A11ress = {{ill|Ōita Prefecture|ja|大分県|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Nakatsu, Ōita|ja|中津市|qq=draft}}, Ōaza Osada 209 | coordinates = {{coord|33|34|2.2|N|131|13|5.1|E|region:JP-44_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | Deities = {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇|qq=draft}}
{{ill|Hime-no-Kami|ja|比売大神|lt=Hime-no-Ōkami|1=Himegami|3=比売神|qq=draft}}
{{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|息長帯比売命|qq=draft}} | Status = Former [[Prefectural Shrine]] (県社) | Established = [[Shōwa era (Japan)|Shōwa era]] (9th century) | Main Hall = {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造|qq=draft}} | Alternate name = Ōsada Hachiman Shrine (大貞八幡宮) | Festivals = April 21 (festival) | Map = {{location map|Japan Oita|label=Komo Shrine|lat_deg=33|lat_min=34|lat_sec=2.2|lon_deg=131|lon_min=13|lon_sec=5.1|position=top|width=210|float=center|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=210|coord={{coord|33|34|2.2|N|131|13|5.1|E}}}} }} '''Komo Shrine''' (薦神社) is a {{ill|Hachiman Shrine|ja|八幡宮|qq=draft}} located in {{ill|Nakatsu, Ōita|ja|中津市|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Ōita Prefecture|ja|大分県|qq=draft}}. It is also referred to as the Ōsada Hachiman Shrine (大貞八幡宮). The shrine features a sacred triangular pond, Misumi Pond (三角池, also historically written as 御澄池), representing the inner shrine, while the sacred hall is considered the outer shrine. This ancient shrine is said to be the progenitor of {{ill|Usa Shrine|ja|宇佐神宮|qq=draft}}, the primary shrine of all Hachiman shrines in Japan, with origins dating to the {{ill|Jōwa (Heian period|lt=Jōwa era|ja|承和|qq=draft}} (834–848). For this reason it is often called "Moto-Usa" == Enshrined Deities == * '''Main Hall''': ''' {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇|qq=draft}} (Hachiman Ōkami) ''' {{ill|Hime-no-Kami|ja|比咩大神|qq=draft}} (The Three Go11esses of Munakata) ''' {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|息長帯比売命|qq=draft}} * '''Yasaka Shrine (Gion Shrine)''': ''' {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞嗚尊|qq=draft}} (Susanoo-no-Mikoto) * '''Wakamiya Shrine''': ''' Offspring of Emperor Ōjin Hime-no-Kami refers to the {{ill|Three Go11esses of Munakata|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=Three Munakata Go11esses|qq=draft}}, represented by: * '''Tagorihime-no-Mikoto''' (沖津宮 at {{ill|Okinoshima|ja|沖ノ島|en|qq=draft}}) * '''Tatsutsuhime-no-Mikoto''' (中津宮 at {{ill|Ōshima (Fukuoka)|ja|大島_(福岡県)|fa|اوشیما (استان فوکوئوکا)|ceb|Ō_Shima_(pulo_sa_Hapon,_Fukuoka-ken)|qq=draft}}) * '''Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto''' (辺津宮 at {{ill|Munakata Taisha|ja|宗像市田島|qq=draft}}). According to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|qq=draft}}, Volume 1, Kami Age, Chapter 6, Variation 3, the Three Go11esses were said to have descended at Usa Island in Ashihara-no-Nakatsukuni, marking their connection to this sacred site. == Shrine Crest == The shrine uses the "Single Tomoe" crest (一つ巴). Unlike the "Triple Tomoe" of Usa Shrine, the Single Tomoe indicates Komo Shrine's status as a progenitor shrine. Some scholars suggest that the shrine originally enshrined one of the Three Go11esses, possibly Tatsutsuhime-no-Mikoto, rather than all three. == History == In {{ill|Yōrō|ja|養老|qq=draft}} 4 (720), during the {{ill|Hayato Rebellion|ja|隼人の反乱|qq=draft}}, the forces led by {{ill|Ōtomo no Tabito|ja|大伴旅人|qq=draft}} and Usa's "Divine Army," under Karashima Haduome, used sacred reed mats (薦枕, Komomakura) from Misumi Pond as a divine artifact to quell the rebellion. The Komokari Festival commemorates this event and is still performed every six years as part of the {{ill|Usa Shrine Procession|ja|宇佐神宮行幸会|qq=draft}} by the Karashima clan. Komo Shrine and Misumi Pond were designated as: * '''Ōita Prefectural Historic Site''' (March 30, 1976) * '''Ōita Prefectural Natural Monument''' for its aquatic and wetland plant communities. The Shinmon Gate, constructed by {{ill|Hosokawa Tadaoki|ja|細川忠興|qq=draft}} in {{ill|Genna|ja|元和|qq=draft}} 8 (1622), is a {{ill|National Treasure of Japan|ja|重要文化財|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)|2=ja|3=重要文化財|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|4=ko|5=일본의_중요문화재|qq=draft}}. The shrine buildings date to the late Edo period. == Annual Rituals == * '''February 11''': Chinyoku Festival and Goshinkō-e (Demon Banishing Ceremony) * '''April 21''': festival * '''Third Saturday and Sunday in September''': Nakashū Festival (Sacred Procession) == Access == * From {{ill|Kokura Station|ja|小倉駅|qq=draft}}: Take the {{ill|Sonic (train)|ja|ソニック|qq=draft}}, a limited express train on the {{ill|Nippō Main Line|ja|日豊本線|qq=draft}}, to {{ill|Nakatsu Station|ja|中津駅|qq=draft}} (approximately 30 minutes). A 15-minute drive from the station. * By Bus: From Nakatsu Station South Exit, take a {{ill|Daiwa Kotsu|ja|大交北部バス|qq=draft}} bound for Tanaka or Shōnen Gakuin-Kansha-mae and alight at "Komo Shrine" (approximately 20 minutes). * By Car: Approximately 15 minutes from the Nakatsu IC on the {{ill|Nippō Expressway|ja|日豊自動車道|qq=draft}}. == References == * ''Photographic Collection: Komo Shrine, Shinmon Volume,'' Komo Culture Research Institute, 1999 * Kenichi Tanigawa (Ed.), ''Japanese Gods and Sacred Sites, Volume 1: Kyūshū,'' Hakusuisha, 1984, ISBN 4-560-02211-9 * ''Cultural Properties of Ōita Prefecture,'' Ōita Prefectural Board of Education, 1991 == See Also == * {{ill|Hata clan|ja|秦氏|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Daigo Hisa|ja|大悟法久志|qq=draft}} == External Links == * [http://www.komojinja.jp/ Official Website of Komo Shrine] {{Shinto navbox}} {{translated page|ja|薦神社|version=101324642|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Komo Shrine (Oita)]] [[Category:Shrines in Ōita Prefecture]] [[Category:Hachiman Shrines]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Shrines dedicated to Empress Jingū]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Historic Sites in Ōita Prefecture]] [[Category:Natural Monuments in Ōita Prefecture]] [[Category:Edo Period architecture]] [[Category:17th-century architecture]] [[Category:Nakatsu, Ōita]] [[Category:Komo Shrine]] }} [[sn:Komo Shrine (Oita)]] [[ja:薦神社]]

For this reason it is often called "Moto-Usa" == Enshrined Deities == * '''Main Hall''': ''' {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Ōjin|qq=}} (Hachiman Ōkami) ''' {{ill|Hime-no-Kami|ja|比咩大神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hime-no-Kami|qq=}} (The Three Go11esses of Munakata) ''' {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|息長帯比売命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Jingū|qq=}} * '''Yasaka Shrine (Gion Shrine)''': ''' {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞嗚尊|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=}} (Susanoo-no-Mikoto) * '''Wakamiya Shrine''': ''' Offspring of Emperor Ōjin Hime-no-Kami refers to the {{ill|Three Go11esses of Munakata|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=Three Munakata Go11esses|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Go11esses of Munakata|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=}}, represented by: * '''Tagorihime-no-Mikoto''' (沖津宮 at {{ill|Okinoshima|ja|沖ノ島|en|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okinoshima|qq=}}) * '''Tatsutsuhime-no-Mikoto''' (中津宮 at {{ill|Ōshima (Fukuoka)|ja|大島_(福岡県)|fa|اوشیما (استان فوکوئوکا)|ceb|Ō_Shima_(pulo_sa_Hapon,_Fukuoka-ken)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōshima (Fukuoka)|qq=}}) * '''Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto''' (辺津宮 at {{ill|Munakata Taisha|ja|宗像市田島|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata Taisha|qq=}}). According to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Shoki|qq=}}, Volume 1, Kami Age, Chapter 6, Variation 3, the Three Go11esses were said to have descended at Usa Island in Ashihara-no-Nakatsukuni, marking their connection to this sacred site. == Shrine Crest == The shrine uses the "Single Tomoe" crest (一つ巴). Unlike the "Triple Tomoe" of Usa Shrine, the Single Tomoe indicates Komo Shrine's status as a progenitor shrine. Some scholars suggest that the shrine originally enshrined one of the Three Go11esses, possibly Tatsutsuhime-no-Mikoto, rather than all three. == History == In {{ill|Yōrō|ja|養老|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yōrō|qq=}} 4 (720), during the {{ill|Hayato Rebellion|ja|隼人の反乱|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayato Rebellion|qq=}}, the forces led by {{ill|Ōtomo no Tabito|ja|大伴旅人|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Tabito|qq=}} and Usa's "Divine Army," under Karashima Haduome, used sacred reed mats (薦枕, Komomakura) from Misumi Pond as a divine artifact to quell the rebellion. The Komokari Festival commemorates this event and is still performed every six years as part of the {{ill|Usa Shrine Procession|ja|宇佐神宮行幸会|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usa Shrine Procession|qq=}} by the Karashima clan. Komo Shrine and Misumi Pond were designated as: * '''Ōita Prefectural Historic Site''' (March 30, 1976) * '''Ōita Prefectural Natural Monument''' for its aquatic and wetland plant communities. The Shinmon Gate, constructed by {{ill|Hosokawa Tadaoki|ja|細川忠興|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hosokawa Tadaoki|qq=}} in {{ill|Genna|ja|元和|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genna|qq=}} 8 (1622), is a {{ill|National Treasure of Japan|ja|重要文化財|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)|2=ja|3=重要文化財|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|4=ko|5=일본의_중요문화재|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Treasure of Japan|13=User:Immanuelle/Important Cultural Property (Japan)|qq=}}. The shrine buildings date to the late Edo period. == Annual Rituals == * '''February 11''': Chinyoku Festival and Goshinkō-e (Demon Banishing Ceremony) * '''April 21''': festival * '''Third Saturday and Sunday in September''': Nakashū Festival (Sacred Procession) == Access == * From {{ill|Kokura Station|ja|小倉駅|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokura Station|qq=}}: Take the {{ill|Sonic (train)|ja|ソニック|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sonic (train)|qq=}}, a limited express train on the {{ill|Nippō Main Line|ja|日豊本線|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nippō Main Line|qq=}}, to {{ill|Nakatsu Station|ja|中津駅|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatsu Station|qq=}} (approximately 30 minutes). A 15-minute drive from the station. * By Bus: From Nakatsu Station South Exit, take a {{ill|Daiwa Kotsu|ja|大交北部バス|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daiwa Kotsu|qq=}} bound for Tanaka or Shōnen Gakuin-Kansha-mae and alight at "Komo Shrine" (approximately 20 minutes). * By Car: Approximately 15 minutes from the Nakatsu IC on the {{ill|Nippō Expressway|ja|日豊自動車道|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nippō Expressway|qq=}}. == References == * ''Photographic Collection: Komo Shrine, Shinmon Volume,'' Komo Culture Research Institute, 1999 * Kenichi Tanigawa (Ed.), ''Japanese Gods and Sacred Sites, Volume 1: Kyūshū,'' Hakusuisha, 1984, ISBN 4-560-02211-9 * ''Cultural Properties of Ōita Prefecture,'' Ōita Prefectural Board of Education, 1991 == See Also == * {{ill|Hata clan|ja|秦氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hata clan|qq=}} * {{ill|Daigo Hisa|ja|大悟法久志|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daigo Hisa|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://www.komojinja.jp/ Official Website of Komo Shrine] {{Shinto navbox}} {{translated page|ja|薦神社|version=101324642|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Komo Shrine (Oita)]] [[Category:Shrines in Ōita Prefecture]] [[Category:Hachiman Shrines]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Shrines dedicated to Empress Jingū]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Historic Sites in Ōita Prefecture]] [[Category:Natural Monuments in Ōita Prefecture]] [[Category:Edo Period architecture]] [[Category:17th-century architecture]] [[Category:Nakatsu, Ōita]] [[Category:Komo Shrine]] }} [[sn:Komo Shrine (Oita)]] [[ja:薦神社]]

The shrine buildings date to the late Edo period. == Annual Rituals == * '''February 11''': Chinyoku Festival and Goshinkō-e (Demon Banishing Ceremony) * '''April 21''': festival * '''Third Saturday and Sunday in September''': Nakashū Festival (Sacred Procession) == Access == * From {{ill|Kokura Station|ja|小倉駅|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokura Station|qq=}}: Take the {{ill|Sonic (train)|ja|ソニック|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sonic (train)|qq=}}, a limited express train on the {{ill|Nippō Main Line|ja|日豊本線|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nippō Main Line|qq=}}, to {{ill|Nakatsu Station|ja|中津駅|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatsu Station|qq=}} (approximately 30 minutes). A 15-minute drive from the station. * By Bus: From Nakatsu Station South Exit, take a {{ill|Daiwa Kotsu|ja|大交北部バス|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daiwa Kotsu|qq=}} bound for Tanaka or Shōnen Gakuin-Kansha-mae and alight at "Komo Shrine" (approximately 20 minutes). * By Car: Approximately 15 minutes from the Nakatsu IC on the {{ill|Nippō Expressway|ja|日豊自動車道|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nippō Expressway|qq=}}. == References == * ''Photographic Collection: Komo Shrine, Shinmon Volume,'' Komo Culture Research Institute, 1999 * Kenichi Tanigawa (Ed.), ''Japanese Gods and Sacred Sites, Volume 1: Kyūshū,'' Hakusuisha, 1984, ISBN 4-560-02211-9 * ''Cultural Properties of Ōita Prefecture,'' Ōita Prefectural Board of Education, 1991 == See Also == * {{ill|Hata clan|ja|秦氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hata clan|qq=}} * {{ill|Daigo Hisa|ja|大悟法久志|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daigo Hisa|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://www.komojinja.jp/ Official Website of Komo Shrine] {{Shinto navbox}} {{translated page|ja|薦神社|version=101324642|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Shrines in Ōita Prefecture]] [[Category:Hachiman Shrines]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Shrines dedicated to Empress Jingū]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Historic Sites in Ōita Prefecture]] [[Category:Natural Monuments in Ōita Prefecture]] [[Category:Edo Period architecture]] [[Category:17th-century architecture]] [[Category:Nakatsu, Ōita]] }} [[sn:Komo Shrine (Oita)]] [[ja:薦神社]]

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Komo Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Komo.jpg|280px|Main Gate]]
Shinmon (Sacred Gate) | A11ress = {{ill|Ōita Prefecture|ja|大分県|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōita Prefecture|qq=}}, {{ill|Nakatsu, Ōita|ja|中津市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatsu, Ōita|qq=}}, Ōaza Osada 209 | coordinates = {{coord|33|34|2.2|N|131|13|5.1|E|region:JP-44_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | Deities = {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Ōjin|qq=}}
{{ill|Hime-no-Kami|ja|比売大神|lt=Hime-no-Ōkami|1=Himegami|3=比売神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hime-no-Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Himegami|qq=}}
{{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|息長帯比売命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Jingū|qq=}} | Status = Former [[Prefectural Shrine]] (県社) | Established = [[Shōwa era (Japan)|Shōwa era]] (9th century) | Main Hall = {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagare-zukuri|qq=}} | Alternate name = Ōsada Hachiman Shrine (大貞八幡宮) | Festivals = April 21 (festival) | Map = {{location map|Japan Oita|label=Komo Shrine|lat_deg=33|lat_min=34|lat_sec=2.2|lon_deg=131|lon_min=13|lon_sec=5.1|position=top|width=210|float=center|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=210|coord={{coord|33|34|2.2|N|131|13|5.1|E}}}} }} '''Komo Shrine''' (薦神社) is a {{ill|Hachiman shrine|lt=Hachiman shrine|lt=Hachiman Shrine|ja|八幡宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman Shrine|qq=}} located in {{ill|Nakatsu, Ōita|ja|中津市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatsu, Ōita|qq=}}, {{ill|Ōita Prefecture|ja|大分県|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōita Prefecture|qq=}}. It is also referred to as the Ōsada Hachiman Shrine (大貞八幡宮). The shrine features a sacred triangular pond, Misumi Pond (三角池, also historically written as 御澄池), representing the inner shrine, while the sacred hall is considered the outer shrine. This ancient shrine is said to be the progenitor of {{ill|Usa Jingū|lt=Usa Jingū|lt=Usa Shrine|ja|宇佐神宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usa Shrine|qq=}}, the primary shrine of all Hachiman shrines in Japan, with origins dating to the {{ill|Jōwa (Heian period|lt=Jōwa era|ja|承和|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōwa (Heian period|qq=}} (834–848). For this reason it is often called "Moto-Usa" == Enshrined Deities == * '''Main Hall''': ''' {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Ōjin|qq=}} (Hachiman Ōkami) ''' {{ill|Hime-no-Kami|ja|比咩大神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hime-no-Kami|qq=}} (The Three Go11esses of Munakata) ''' {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|息長帯比売命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Jingū|qq=}} * '''Yasaka Shrine (Gion Shrine)''': ''' {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞嗚尊|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=}} (Susanoo-no-Mikoto) * '''Wakamiya Shrine''': ''' Offspring of Emperor Ōjin Hime-no-Kami refers to the {{ill|Three Go11esses of Munakata|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=Three Munakata Go11esses|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Go11esses of Munakata|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=}}, represented by: * '''Tagorihime-no-Mikoto''' (沖津宮 at {{ill|Okinoshima|ja|沖ノ島|en|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okinoshima|qq=}}) * '''Tatsutsuhime-no-Mikoto''' (中津宮 at {{ill|Ōshima (Fukuoka)|ja|大島_(福岡県)|fa|اوشیما (استان فوکوئوکا)|ceb|Ō_Shima_(pulo_sa_Hapon,_Fukuoka-ken)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōshima (Fukuoka)|qq=}}) * '''Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto''' (辺津宮 at {{ill|Munakata Taisha|ja|宗像市田島|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata Taisha|qq=}}). According to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Shoki|qq=}}, Volume 1, Kami Age, Chapter 6, Variation 3, the Three Go11esses were said to have descended at Usa Island in Ashihara-no-Nakatsukuni, marking their connection to this sacred site. == Shrine Crest == The shrine uses the "Single Tomoe" crest (一つ巴). Unlike the "Triple Tomoe" of Usa Shrine, the Single Tomoe indicates Komo Shrine's status as a progenitor shrine. Some scholars suggest that the shrine originally enshrined one of the Three Go11esses, possibly Tatsutsuhime-no-Mikoto, rather than all three. == History == In {{ill|Yōrō|ja|養老|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yōrō|qq=}} 4 (720), during the {{ill|Hayato Rebellion|ja|隼人の反乱|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayato Rebellion|qq=}}, the forces led by {{ill|Ōtomo no Tabito|ja|大伴旅人|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Tabito|qq=}} and Usa's "Divine Army," under Karashima Haduome, used sacred reed mats (薦枕, Komomakura) from Misumi Pond as a divine artifact to quell the rebellion. The Komokari Festival commemorates this event and is still performed every six years as part of the {{ill|Usa Shrine Procession|ja|宇佐神宮行幸会|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usa Shrine Procession|qq=}} by the Karashima clan. Komo Shrine and Misumi Pond were designated as: * '''Ōita Prefectural Historic Site''' (March 30, 1976) * '''Ōita Prefectural Natural Monument''' for its aquatic and wetland plant communities. The Shinmon Gate, constructed by {{ill|Hosokawa Tadaoki|ja|細川忠興|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hosokawa Tadaoki|qq=}} in {{ill|Genna|ja|元和|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genna|qq=}} 8 (1622), is a {{ill|National Treasure of Japan|ja|重要文化財|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)|2=ja|3=重要文化財|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|4=ko|5=일본의_중요문화재|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Treasure of Japan|13=User:Immanuelle/Important Cultural Property (Japan)|qq=}}. The shrine buildings date to the late Edo period. == Annual Rituals == * '''February 11''': Chinyoku Festival and Goshinkō-e (Demon Banishing Ceremony) * '''April 21''': festival * '''Third Saturday and Sunday in September''': Nakashū Festival (Sacred Procession) == Access == * From {{ill|Kokura Station|ja|小倉駅|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokura Station|qq=}}: Take the {{ill|Sonic (train)|ja|ソニック|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sonic (train)|qq=}}, a limited express train on the {{ill|Nippō Main Line|ja|日豊本線|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nippō Main Line|qq=}}, to {{ill|Nakatsu Station|ja|中津駅|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatsu Station|qq=}} (approximately 30 minutes). A 15-minute drive from the station. * By Bus: From Nakatsu Station South Exit, take a {{ill|Daiwa Kotsu|ja|大交北部バス|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daiwa Kotsu|qq=}} bound for Tanaka or Shōnen Gakuin-Kansha-mae and alight at "Komo Shrine" (approximately 20 minutes). * By Car: Approximately 15 minutes from the Nakatsu IC on the {{ill|Nippō Expressway|ja|日豊自動車道|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nippō Expressway|qq=}}. == References == * ''Photographic Collection: Komo Shrine, Shinmon Volume,'' Komo Culture Research Institute, 1999 * Kenichi Tanigawa (Ed.), ''Japanese Gods and Sacred Sites, Volume 1: Kyūshū,'' Hakusuisha, 1984, ISBN 4-560-02211-9 * ''Cultural Properties of Ōita Prefecture,'' Ōita Prefectural Board of Education, 1991 == See Also == * {{ill|Hata clan|ja|秦氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hata clan|qq=}} * {{ill|Daigo Hisa|ja|大悟法久志|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daigo Hisa|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://www.komojinja.jp/ Official Website of Komo Shrine] {{Shinto navbox}} {{translated page|ja|薦神社|version=101324642|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Shrines in Ōita Prefecture]] [[Category:Hachiman Shrines]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Shrines dedicated to Empress Jingū]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Historic Sites in Ōita Prefecture]] [[Category:Natural Monuments in Ōita Prefecture]] [[Category:Edo Period architecture]] [[Category:17th-century architecture]] [[Category:Nakatsu, Ōita]] }} [[sn:Komo Shrine (Oita)]] [[ja:薦神社]]

The shrine features a sacred triangular pond, Misumi Pond (三角池, also historically written as 御澄池), representing the inner shrine, while the sacred hall is considered the outer shrine. This ancient shrine is said to be the progenitor of [[:en:Usa Jingū|Usa Shrine]], the primary shrine of all Hachiman shrines in Japan, with origins dating to the [[:en:Jōwa|Jōwa era]] (834–848). For this reason it is often called "Moto-Usa" == Enshrined Deities == * '''Main Hall''': ''' [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]] (Hachiman Ōkami) ''' {{ill|Hime-no-Kami|ja|比咩大神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hime-no-Kami|qq=}} (The Three Go11esses of Munakata) ''' {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|息長帯比売命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Jingū|qq=}} * '''Yasaka Shrine (Gion Shrine)''': ''' {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞嗚尊|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=}} (Susanoo-no-Mikoto) * '''Wakamiya Shrine''': ''' Offspring of Emperor Ōjin Hime-no-Kami refers to the {{ill|Three Go11esses of Munakata|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=Three Munakata Go11esses|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Go11esses of Munakata|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=}}, represented by: * '''Tagorihime-no-Mikoto''' (沖津宮 at [[:en:Okinoshima (Fukuoka)|Okinoshima]]) * '''Tatsutsuhime-no-Mikoto''' (中津宮 at {{ill|Ōshima (Fukuoka)|ja|大島_(福岡県)|fa|اوشیما (استان فوکوئوکا)|ceb|Ō_Shima_(pulo_sa_Hapon,_Fukuoka-ken)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōshima (Fukuoka)|qq=}}) * '''Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto''' (辺津宮 at {{ill|Munakata Taisha|ja|宗像市田島|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata Taisha|qq=}}). According to the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], Volume 1, Kami Age, Chapter 6, Variation 3, the Three Go11esses were said to have descended at Usa Island in Ashihara-no-Nakatsukuni, marking their connection to this sacred site. == Shrine Crest == The shrine uses the "Single Tomoe" crest (一つ巴). Unlike the "Triple Tomoe" of Usa Shrine, the Single Tomoe indicates Komo Shrine's status as a progenitor shrine. Some scholars suggest that the shrine originally enshrined one of the Three Go11esses, possibly Tatsutsuhime-no-Mikoto, rather than all three. == History == In [[:en:Yōrō|Yōrō]] 4 (720), during the [[:en:Hayato rebellion|Hayato Rebellion]], the forces led by [[:en:Ōtomo no Tabito|Ōtomo no Tabito]] and Usa's "Divine Army," under Karashima Haduome, used sacred reed mats (薦枕, Komomakura) from Misumi Pond as a divine artifact to quell the rebellion. The Komokari Festival commemorates this event and is still performed every six years as part of the {{ill|Usa Shrine Procession|ja|宇佐神宮行幸会|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usa Shrine Procession|qq=}} by the Karashima clan. Komo Shrine and Misumi Pond were designated as: * '''Ōita Prefectural Historic Site''' (March 30, 1976) * '''Ōita Prefectural Natural Monument''' for its aquatic and wetland plant communities. The Shinmon Gate, constructed by [[:en:Hosokawa Tadaoki|Hosokawa Tadaoki]] in [[:en:Yuanhe|Genna]] 8 (1622), is a [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]]. The shrine buildings date to the late Edo period. == Annual Rituals == * '''February 11''': Chinyoku Festival and Goshinkō-e (Demon Banishing Ceremony) * '''April 21''': festival * '''Third Saturday and Sunday in September''': Nakashū Festival (Sacred Procession) == Access == * From {{ill|Kokura Station|ja|小倉駅|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokura Station|qq=}}: Take the [[:en:Sonic|Sonic (train)]], a limited express train on the [[:en:Nippō Main Line|Nippō Main Line]], to [[:en:Nakatsu Station|Nakatsu Station]] (approximately 30 minutes). A 15-minute drive from the station. * By Bus: From Nakatsu Station South Exit, take a {{ill|Daiwa Kotsu|ja|大交北部バス|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daiwa Kotsu|qq=}} bound for Tanaka or Shōnen Gakuin-Kansha-mae and alight at "Komo Shrine" (approximately 20 minutes). * By Car: Approximately 15 minutes from the Nakatsu IC on the {{ill|Nippō Expressway|ja|日豊自動車道|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nippō Expressway|qq=}}. == References == * ''Photographic Collection: Komo Shrine, Shinmon Volume,'' Komo Culture Research Institute, 1999 * Kenichi Tanigawa (Ed.), ''Japanese Gods and Sacred Sites, Volume 1: Kyūshū,'' Hakusuisha, 1984, ISBN 4-560-02211-9 * ''Cultural Properties of Ōita Prefecture,'' Ōita Prefectural Board of Education, 1991 == See Also == * [[:en:Hata clan|Hata clan]] * {{ill|Daigo Hisa|ja|大悟法久志|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daigo Hisa|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://www.komojinja.jp/ Official Website of Komo Shrine] {{Shinto navbox}} {{translated page|ja|薦神社|version=101324642|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Shrines in Ōita Prefecture]] [[Category:Hachiman Shrines]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Shrines dedicated to Empress Jingū]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Historic Sites in Ōita Prefecture]] [[Category:Natural Monuments in Ōita Prefecture]] [[Category:Edo Period architecture]] [[Category:17th-century architecture]] [[Category:Nakatsu, Ōita]] }} [[sn:Komo Shrine (Oita)]] [[ja:薦神社]] [[Category:Buildings and structures completed in the 17th century]]

For this reason it is often called "Moto-Usa" == Enshrined Deities == * '''Main Hall''': ''' [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]] (Hachiman Ōkami) ''' {{ill|Hime-no-Kami|ja|比咩大神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hime-no-Kami|qq=}} (The Three Go11esses of Munakata) ''' {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|息長帯比売命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Jingū|qq=}} * '''Yasaka Shrine (Gion Shrine)''': ''' {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞嗚尊|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=}} (Susanoo-no-Mikoto) * '''Wakamiya Shrine''': ''' Offspring of Emperor Ōjin Hime-no-Kami refers to the {{ill|Three Go11esses of Munakata|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=Three Munakata Go11esses|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Go11esses of Munakata|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=}}, represented by: * '''Tagorihime-no-Mikoto''' (沖津宮 at [[:en:Okinoshima (Fukuoka)|Okinoshima]]) * '''Tatsutsuhime-no-Mikoto''' (中津宮 at {{ill|Ōshima (Fukuoka)|ja|大島_(福岡県)|fa|اوشیما (استان فوکوئوکا)|ceb|Ō_Shima_(pulo_sa_Hapon,_Fukuoka-ken)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōshima (Fukuoka)|qq=}}) * '''Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto''' (辺津宮 at {{ill|Munakata Taisha|ja|宗像市田島|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata Taisha|qq=}}). According to the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], Volume 1, Kami Age, Chapter 6, Variation 3, the Three Go11esses were said to have descended at Usa Island in Ashihara-no-Nakatsukuni, marking their connection to this sacred site. == Shrine Crest == The shrine uses the "Single Tomoe" crest (一つ巴). Unlike the "Triple Tomoe" of Usa Shrine, the Single Tomoe indicates Komo Shrine's status as a progenitor shrine. Some scholars suggest that the shrine originally enshrined one of the Three Go11esses, possibly Tatsutsuhime-no-Mikoto, rather than all three. == History == In [[:en:Yōrō|Yōrō]] 4 (720), during the [[:en:Hayato rebellion|Hayato Rebellion]], the forces led by [[:en:Ōtomo no Tabito|Ōtomo no Tabito]] and Usa's "Divine Army," under Karashima Haduome, used sacred reed mats (薦枕, Komomakura) from Misumi Pond as a divine artifact to quell the rebellion. The Komokari Festival commemorates this event and is still performed every six years as part of the {{ill|Usa Shrine Procession|ja|宇佐神宮行幸会|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usa Shrine Procession|qq=}} by the Karashima clan. Komo Shrine and Misumi Pond were designated as: * '''Ōita Prefectural Historic Site''' (March 30, 1976) * '''Ōita Prefectural Natural Monument''' for its aquatic and wetland plant communities. The Shinmon Gate, constructed by [[:en:Hosokawa Tadaoki|Hosokawa Tadaoki]] in [[:en:Yuanhe|Genna]] 8 (1622), is a [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]]. The shrine buildings date to the late Edo period. == Annual Rituals == * '''February 11''': Chinyoku Festival and Goshinkō-e (Demon Banishing Ceremony) * '''April 21''': festival * '''Third Saturday and Sunday in September''': Nakashū Festival (Sacred Procession) == Access == * From {{ill|Kokura Station|ja|小倉駅|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokura Station|qq=}}: Take the [[:en:Sonic|Sonic (train)]], a limited express train on the [[:en:Nippō Main Line|Nippō Main Line]], to [[:en:Nakatsu Station|Nakatsu Station]] (approximately 30 minutes). A 15-minute drive from the station. * By Bus: From Nakatsu Station South Exit, take a {{ill|Daiwa Kotsu|ja|大交北部バス|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daiwa Kotsu|qq=}} bound for Tanaka or Shōnen Gakuin-Kansha-mae and alight at "Komo Shrine" (approximately 20 minutes). * By Car: Approximately 15 minutes from the Nakatsu IC on the {{ill|Nippō Expressway|ja|日豊自動車道|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nippō Expressway|qq=}}. == References == * ''Photographic Collection: Komo Shrine, Shinmon Volume,'' Komo Culture Research Institute, 1999 * Kenichi Tanigawa (Ed.), ''Japanese Gods and Sacred Sites, Volume 1: Kyūshū,'' Hakusuisha, 1984, ISBN 4-560-02211-9 * ''Cultural Properties of Ōita Prefecture,'' Ōita Prefectural Board of Education, 1991 == See Also == * [[:en:Hata clan|Hata clan]] * {{ill|Daigo Hisa|ja|大悟法久志|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daigo Hisa|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://www.komojinja.jp/ Official Website of Komo Shrine] {{Shinto navbox}} {{translated page|ja|薦神社|version=101324642|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Shrines in Ōita Prefecture]] [[Category:Hachiman Shrines]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Shrines dedicated to Empress Jingū]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Historic Sites in Ōita Prefecture]] [[Category:Natural Monuments in Ōita Prefecture]] [[Category:Edo Period architecture]] [[Category:17th-century architecture]] [[Category:Nakatsu, Ōita]] }} [[sn:Komo Shrine (Oita)]] [[ja:薦神社]] [[Category:Buildings and structures completed in the 17th century]] [[Category:中津市の建築物]] [[Category:中津市の歴史]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]]

For this reason it is often called "Moto-Usa" == Enshrined Deities == * '''Main Hall''': ''' [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]] (Hachiman Ōkami) ''' {{ill|Hime-no-Kami|ja|比咩大神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hime-no-Kami|qq=}} (The Three Go11esses of Munakata) ''' {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|息長帯比売命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Jingū|qq=}} * '''Yasaka Shrine (Gion Shrine)''': ''' {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞嗚尊|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=}} (Susanoo-no-Mikoto) * '''Wakamiya Shrine''': ''' Offspring of Emperor Ōjin Hime-no-Kami refers to the {{ill|Three Go11esses of Munakata|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=Three Munakata Go11esses|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Go11esses of Munakata|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=}}, represented by: * '''Tagorihime-no-Mikoto''' (沖津宮 at [[:en:Okinoshima (Fukuoka)|Okinoshima]]) * '''Tatsutsuhime-no-Mikoto''' (中津宮 at {{ill|Ōshima (Fukuoka)|ja|大島_(福岡県)|fa|اوشیما (استان فوکوئوکا)|ceb|Ō_Shima_(pulo_sa_Hapon,_Fukuoka-ken)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōshima (Fukuoka)|qq=}}) * '''Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto''' (辺津宮 at {{ill|Munakata Taisha|ja|宗像市田島|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata Taisha|qq=}}). According to the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], Volume 1, Kami Age, Chapter 6, Variation 3, the Three Go11esses were said to have descended at Usa Island in Ashihara-no-Nakatsukuni, marking their connection to this sacred site. == Shrine Crest == The shrine uses the "Single Tomoe" crest (一つ巴). Unlike the "Triple Tomoe" of Usa Shrine, the Single Tomoe indicates Komo Shrine's status as a progenitor shrine. Some scholars suggest that the shrine originally enshrined one of the Three Go11esses, possibly Tatsutsuhime-no-Mikoto, rather than all three. == History == In [[:en:Yōrō|Yōrō]] 4 (720), during the [[:en:Hayato rebellion|Hayato Rebellion]], the forces led by [[:en:Ōtomo no Tabito|Ōtomo no Tabito]] and Usa's "Divine Army," under Karashima Haduome, used sacred reed mats (薦枕, Komomakura) from Misumi Pond as a divine artifact to quell the rebellion. The Komokari Festival commemorates this event and is still performed every six years as part of the {{ill|Usa Shrine Procession|ja|宇佐神宮行幸会|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usa Shrine Procession|qq=}} by the Karashima clan. Komo Shrine and Misumi Pond were designated as: * '''Ōita Prefectural Historic Site''' (March 30, 1976) * '''Ōita Prefectural Natural Monument''' for its aquatic and wetland plant communities. The Shinmon Gate, constructed by [[:en:Hosokawa Tadaoki|Hosokawa Tadaoki]] in [[:en:Yuanhe|Genna]] 8 (1622), is a [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]]. The shrine buildings date to the late Edo period. == Annual Rituals == * '''February 11''': Chinyoku Festival and Goshinkō-e (Demon Banishing Ceremony) * '''April 21''': festival * '''Third Saturday and Sunday in September''': Nakashū Festival (Sacred Procession) == Access == * From {{ill|Kokura Station|ja|小倉駅|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokura Station|qq=}}: Take the [[:en:Sonic|Sonic (train)]], a limited express train on the [[:en:Nippō Main Line|Nippō Main Line]], to [[:en:Nakatsu Station|Nakatsu Station]] (approximately 30 minutes). A 15-minute drive from the station. * By Bus: From Nakatsu Station South Exit, take a {{ill|Daiwa Kotsu|ja|大交北部バス|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daiwa Kotsu|qq=}} bound for Tanaka or Shōnen Gakuin-Kansha-mae and alight at "Komo Shrine" (approximately 20 minutes). * By Car: Approximately 15 minutes from the Nakatsu IC on the {{ill|Nippō Expressway|ja|日豊自動車道|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nippō Expressway|qq=}}. == References == * ''Photographic Collection: Komo Shrine, Shinmon Volume,'' Komo Culture Research Institute, 1999 * Kenichi Tanigawa (Ed.), ''Japanese Gods and Sacred Sites, Volume 1: Kyūshū,'' Hakusuisha, 1984, ISBN 4-560-02211-9 * ''Cultural Properties of Ōita Prefecture,'' Ōita Prefectural Board of Education, 1991 == See Also == * [[:en:Hata clan|Hata clan]] * {{ill|Daigo Hisa|ja|大悟法久志|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daigo Hisa|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://www.komojinja.jp/ Official Website of Komo Shrine] {{Shinto navbox}} {{translated page|ja|薦神社|version=101324642|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Shrines in Ōita Prefecture]] [[Category:Hachiman Shrines]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Shrines dedicated to Empress Jingū]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Historic Sites in Ōita Prefecture]] [[Category:Natural Monuments in Ōita Prefecture]] [[Category:Edo Period architecture]] [[Category:17th-century architecture]] [[Category:Nakatsu, Ōita]] }} [[sn:Komo Shrine (Oita)]] [[ja:薦神社]] [[Category:Buildings and structures completed in the 17th century]] [[Category:中津市の建築物]] [[Category:中津市の歴史]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Oita Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ōita Prefecture]] [[Category:大分県の重要文化財]]

For this reason it is often called "Moto-Usa" == Enshrined Deities == * '''Main Hall''': ''' [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]] (Hachiman Ōkami) ''' {{ill|Hime-no-Kami|ja|比咩大神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hime-no-Kami|qq=}} (The Three Go11esses of Munakata) ''' {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|息長帯比売命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Jingū|qq=}} * '''Yasaka Shrine (Gion Shrine)''': ''' {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞嗚尊|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=}} (Susanoo-no-Mikoto) * '''Wakamiya Shrine''': ''' Offspring of Emperor Ōjin Hime-no-Kami refers to the {{ill|Three Go11esses of Munakata|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=Three Munakata Go11esses|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Go11esses of Munakata|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=}}, represented by: * '''Tagorihime-no-Mikoto''' (沖津宮 at [[:en:Okinoshima (Fukuoka)|Okinoshima]]) * '''Tatsutsuhime-no-Mikoto''' (中津宮 at {{ill|Ōshima (Fukuoka)|ja|大島_(福岡県)|fa|اوشیما (استان فوکوئوکا)|ceb|Ō_Shima_(pulo_sa_Hapon,_Fukuoka-ken)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōshima (Fukuoka)|qq=}}) * '''Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto''' (辺津宮 at {{ill|Munakata Taisha|ja|宗像市田島|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata Taisha|qq=}}). According to the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], Volume 1, Kami Age, Chapter 6, Variation 3, the Three Go11esses were said to have descended at Usa Island in Ashihara-no-Nakatsukuni, marking their connection to this sacred site. == Shrine Crest == The shrine uses the "Single Tomoe" crest (一つ巴). Unlike the "Triple Tomoe" of Usa Shrine, the Single Tomoe indicates Komo Shrine's status as a progenitor shrine. Some scholars suggest that the shrine originally enshrined one of the Three Go11esses, possibly Tatsutsuhime-no-Mikoto, rather than all three. == History == In [[:en:Yōrō|Yōrō]] 4 (720), during the [[:en:Hayato rebellion|Hayato Rebellion]], the forces led by [[:en:Ōtomo no Tabito|Ōtomo no Tabito]] and Usa's "Divine Army," under Karashima Haduome, used sacred reed mats (薦枕, Komomakura) from Misumi Pond as a divine artifact to quell the rebellion. The Komokari Festival commemorates this event and is still performed every six years as part of the {{ill|Usa Shrine Procession|ja|宇佐神宮行幸会|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usa Shrine Procession|qq=}} by the Karashima clan. Komo Shrine and Misumi Pond were designated as: * '''Ōita Prefectural Historic Site''' (March 30, 1976) * '''Ōita Prefectural Natural Monument''' for its aquatic and wetland plant communities. The Shinmon Gate, constructed by [[:en:Hosokawa Tadaoki|Hosokawa Tadaoki]] in [[:en:Yuanhe|Genna]] 8 (1622), is a [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]]. The shrine buildings date to the late Edo period. == Annual Rituals == * '''February 11''': Chinyoku Festival and Goshinkō-e (Demon Banishing Ceremony) * '''April 21''': festival * '''Third Saturday and Sunday in September''': Nakashū Festival (Sacred Procession) == Access == * From {{ill|Kokura Station|ja|小倉駅|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokura Station|qq=}}: Take the [[:en:Sonic|Sonic (train)]], a limited express train on the [[:en:Nippō Main Line|Nippō Main Line]], to [[:en:Nakatsu Station|Nakatsu Station]] (approximately 30 minutes). A 15-minute drive from the station. * By Bus: From Nakatsu Station South Exit, take a {{ill|Daiwa Kotsu|ja|大交北部バス|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daiwa Kotsu|qq=}} bound for Tanaka or Shōnen Gakuin-Kansha-mae and alight at "Komo Shrine" (approximately 20 minutes). * By Car: Approximately 15 minutes from the Nakatsu IC on the {{ill|Nippō Expressway|ja|日豊自動車道|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nippō Expressway|qq=}}. == References == * ''Photographic Collection: Komo Shrine, Shinmon Volume,'' Komo Culture Research Institute, 1999 * Kenichi Tanigawa (Ed.), ''Japanese Gods and Sacred Sites, Volume 1: Kyūshū,'' Hakusuisha, 1984, ISBN 4-560-02211-9 * ''Cultural Properties of Ōita Prefecture,'' Ōita Prefectural Board of Education, 1991 == See Also == * [[:en:Hata clan|Hata clan]] * {{ill|Daigo Hisa|ja|大悟法久志|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daigo Hisa|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://www.komojinja.jp/ Official Website of Komo Shrine] {{Shinto navbox}} {{translated page|ja|薦神社|version=101324642|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Shrines in Ōita Prefecture]] [[Category:Hachiman Shrines]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Shrines dedicated to Empress Jingū]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Historic Sites in Ōita Prefecture]] [[Category:Natural Monuments in Ōita Prefecture]] [[Category:Edo Period architecture]] [[Category:17th-century architecture]] [[Category:Nakatsu, Ōita]] }} [[sn:Komo Shrine (Oita)]] [[ja:薦神社]] [[Category:Buildings and structures completed in the 17th century]] [[Category:中津市の建築物]] [[Category:中津市の歴史]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Oita Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ōita Prefecture]] [[Category:大分県の重要文化財]] [[Category:Designated historic sites of Oita Prefecture]] [[Category:大分県指定天然記念物]] [[Category:Edo period architecture]]

This ancient shrine is said to be the progenitor of [[:en:Usa Jingū|Usa Shrine]], the primary shrine of all Hachiman shrines in Japan, with origins dating to the [[:en:Jōwa|Jōwa era]] (834–848). For this reason it is often called "Moto-Usa" == Enshrined Deities == * '''Main Hall''': ''' [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]] (Hachiman Ōkami) ''' {{ill|Hime-no-Kami|ja|比咩大神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hime-no-Kami|qq=}} (The Three Go11esses of Munakata) ''' {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|息長帯比売命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Jingū|qq=}} * '''Yasaka Shrine (Gion Shrine)''': ''' {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞嗚尊|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=}} (Susanoo-no-Mikoto) * '''Wakamiya Shrine''': ''' Offspring of Emperor Ōjin Hime-no-Kami refers to the {{ill|Three Go11esses of Munakata|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=Three Munakata Go11esses|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Go11esses of Munakata|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=}}, represented by: * '''Tagorihime-no-Mikoto''' (沖津宮 at [[:en:Okinoshima (Fukuoka)|Okinoshima]]) * '''Tatsutsuhime-no-Mikoto''' (中津宮 at {{ill|Ōshima (Fukuoka)|ja|大島_(福岡県)|fa|اوشیما (استان فوکوئوکا)|ceb|Ō_Shima_(pulo_sa_Hapon,_Fukuoka-ken)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōshima (Fukuoka)|qq=}}) * '''Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto''' (辺津宮 at {{ill|Munakata Taisha|ja|宗像市田島|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata Taisha|qq=}}). According to the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], Volume 1, Kami Age, Chapter 6, Variation 3, the Three Go11esses were said to have descended at Usa Island in Ashihara-no-Nakatsukuni, marking their connection to this sacred site. == Shrine Crest == The shrine uses the "Single Tomoe" crest (一つ巴). Unlike the "Triple Tomoe" of Usa Shrine, the Single Tomoe indicates Komo Shrine's status as a progenitor shrine. Some scholars suggest that the shrine originally enshrined one of the Three Go11esses, possibly Tatsutsuhime-no-Mikoto, rather than all three. == History == In [[:en:Yōrō|Yōrō]] 4 (720), during the [[:en:Hayato rebellion|Hayato Rebellion]], the forces led by [[:en:Ōtomo no Tabito|Ōtomo no Tabito]] and Usa's "Divine Army," under Karashima Haduome, used sacred reed mats (薦枕, Komomakura) from Misumi Pond as a divine artifact to quell the rebellion. The Komokari Festival commemorates this event and is still performed every six years as part of the {{ill|Usa Shrine Procession|ja|宇佐神宮行幸会|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usa Shrine Procession|qq=}} by the Karashima clan. Komo Shrine and Misumi Pond were designated as: * '''Ōita Prefectural Historic Site''' (March 30, 1976) * '''Ōita Prefectural Natural Monument''' for its aquatic and wetland plant communities. The Shinmon Gate, constructed by [[:en:Hosokawa Tadaoki|Hosokawa Tadaoki]] in [[:en:Yuanhe|Genna]] 8 (1622), is a [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]]. The shrine buildings date to the late Edo period. == Annual Rituals == * '''February 11''': Chinyoku Festival and Goshinkō-e (Demon Banishing Ceremony) * '''April 21''': festival * '''Third Saturday and Sunday in September''': Nakashū Festival (Sacred Procession) == Access == * From {{ill|Kokura Station|ja|小倉駅|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokura Station|qq=}}: Take the [[:en:Sonic|Sonic (train)]], a limited express train on the [[:en:Nippō Main Line|Nippō Main Line]], to [[:en:Nakatsu Station|Nakatsu Station]] (approximately 30 minutes). A 15-minute drive from the station. * By Bus: From Nakatsu Station South Exit, take a {{ill|Daiwa Kotsu|ja|大交北部バス|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daiwa Kotsu|qq=}} bound for Tanaka or Shōnen Gakuin-Kansha-mae and alight at "Komo Shrine" (approximately 20 minutes). * By Car: Approximately 15 minutes from the Nakatsu IC on the {{ill|Nippō Expressway|ja|日豊自動車道|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nippō Expressway|qq=}}. == References == * ''Photographic Collection: Komo Shrine, Shinmon Volume,'' Komo Culture Research Institute, 1999 * Kenichi Tanigawa (Ed.), ''Japanese Gods and Sacred Sites, Volume 1: Kyūshū,'' Hakusuisha, 1984, ISBN 4-560-02211-9 * ''Cultural Properties of Ōita Prefecture,'' Ōita Prefectural Board of Education, 1991 == See Also == * [[:en:Hata clan|Hata clan]] * {{ill|Daigo Hisa|ja|大悟法久志|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daigo Hisa|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://www.komojinja.jp/ Official Website of Komo Shrine] {{Shinto navbox}} {{translated page|ja|薦神社|version=101324642|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Shrines in Ōita Prefecture]] [[Category:Hachiman Shrines]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Shrines dedicated to Empress Jingū]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Historic Sites in Ōita Prefecture]] [[Category:Natural Monuments in Ōita Prefecture]] [[Category:Edo Period architecture]] [[Category:17th-century architecture]] [[Category:Nakatsu, Ōita]] }} [[sn:Komo Shrine (Oita)]] [[ja:薦神社]] [[Category:Buildings and structures completed in the 17th century]] [[Category:Buildings in Nakatsu, Oita]] [[Category:中津市の歴史]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Oita Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ōita Prefecture]] [[Category:大分県の重要文化財]] [[Category:Designated historic sites of Oita Prefecture]] [[Category:大分県指定天然記念物]] [[Category:Edo period architecture]] [[Category:Cultural heritage monuments in Japan]]

{{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|息長帯比売命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Jingū|qq=}} | Status = Former [[Prefectural Shrine]] (県社) | Established = [[Shōwa era (Japan)|Shōwa era]] (9th century) | Main Hall = [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] | Alternate name = Ōsada Hachiman Shrine (大貞八幡宮) | Festivals = April 21 (festival) | Map = {{location map|Japan Oita|label=Komo Shrine|lat_deg=33|lat_min=34|lat_sec=2.2|lon_deg=131|lon_min=13|lon_sec=5.1|position=top|width=210|float=center|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=210|coord={{coord|33|34|2.2|N|131|13|5.1|E}}}} }} '''Komo Shrine''' (薦神社) is a [[:en:Hachiman shrine|Hachiman Shrine]] located in [[:en:Nakatsu, Ōita|Nakatsu, Ōita]], [[:en:Ōita Prefecture|Ōita Prefecture]]. It is also referred to as the Ōsada Hachiman Shrine (大貞八幡宮). The shrine features a sacred triangular pond, Misumi Pond (三角池, also historically written as 御澄池), representing the inner shrine, while the sacred hall is considered the outer shrine. This ancient shrine is said to be the progenitor of [[:en:Usa Jingū|Usa Shrine]], the primary shrine of all Hachiman shrines in Japan, with origins dating to the [[:en:Jōwa|Jōwa era]] (834–848). For this reason it is often called "Moto-Usa" == Enshrined Deities == * '''Main Hall''': ''' [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]] (Hachiman Ōkami) ''' {{ill|Hime-no-Kami|ja|比咩大神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hime-no-Kami|qq=}} (The Three Go11esses of Munakata) ''' {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|息長帯比売命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Jingū|qq=}} * '''Yasaka Shrine (Gion Shrine)''': ''' {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞嗚尊|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=}} (Susanoo-no-Mikoto) * '''Wakamiya Shrine''': ''' Offspring of Emperor Ōjin Hime-no-Kami refers to the {{ill|Three Go11esses of Munakata|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=Three Munakata Go11esses|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Go11esses of Munakata|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=}}, represented by: * '''Tagorihime-no-Mikoto''' (沖津宮 at [[:en:Okinoshima (Fukuoka)|Okinoshima]]) * '''Tatsutsuhime-no-Mikoto''' (中津宮 at {{ill|Ōshima (Fukuoka)|ja|大島_(福岡県)|fa|اوشیما (استان فوکوئوکا)|ceb|Ō_Shima_(pulo_sa_Hapon,_Fukuoka-ken)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōshima (Fukuoka)|qq=}}) * '''Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto''' (辺津宮 at {{ill|Munakata Taisha|ja|宗像市田島|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata Taisha|qq=}}). According to the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], Volume 1, Kami Age, Chapter 6, Variation 3, the Three Go11esses were said to have descended at Usa Island in Ashihara-no-Nakatsukuni, marking their connection to this sacred site. == Shrine Crest == The shrine uses the "Single Tomoe" crest (一つ巴). Unlike the "Triple Tomoe" of Usa Shrine, the Single Tomoe indicates Komo Shrine's status as a progenitor shrine. Some scholars suggest that the shrine originally enshrined one of the Three Go11esses, possibly Tatsutsuhime-no-Mikoto, rather than all three. == History == In [[:en:Yōrō|Yōrō]] 4 (720), during the [[:en:Hayato rebellion|Hayato Rebellion]], the forces led by [[:en:Ōtomo no Tabito|Ōtomo no Tabito]] and Usa's "Divine Army," under Karashima Haduome, used sacred reed mats (薦枕, Komomakura) from Misumi Pond as a divine artifact to quell the rebellion. The Komokari Festival commemorates this event and is still performed every six years as part of the {{ill|Usa Shrine Procession|ja|宇佐神宮行幸会|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usa Shrine Procession|qq=}} by the Karashima clan. Komo Shrine and Misumi Pond were designated as: * '''Ōita Prefectural Historic Site''' (March 30, 1976) * '''Ōita Prefectural Natural Monument''' for its aquatic and wetland plant communities. The Shinmon Gate, constructed by [[:en:Hosokawa Tadaoki|Hosokawa Tadaoki]] in [[:en:Yuanhe|Genna]] 8 (1622), is a [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]]. The shrine buildings date to the late Edo period. == Annual Rituals == * '''February 11''': Chinyoku Festival and Goshinkō-e (Demon Banishing Ceremony) * '''April 21''': festival * '''Third Saturday and Sunday in September''': Nakashū Festival (Sacred Procession) == Access == * From {{ill|Kokura Station|ja|小倉駅|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokura Station|qq=}}: Take the [[:en:Sonic|Sonic (train)]], a limited express train on the [[:en:Nippō Main Line|Nippō Main Line]], to [[:en:Nakatsu Station|Nakatsu Station]] (approximately 30 minutes). A 15-minute drive from the station. * By Bus: From Nakatsu Station South Exit, take a {{ill|Daiwa Kotsu|ja|大交北部バス|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daiwa Kotsu|qq=}} bound for Tanaka or Shōnen Gakuin-Kansha-mae and alight at "Komo Shrine" (approximately 20 minutes). * By Car: Approximately 15 minutes from the Nakatsu IC on the {{ill|Nippō Expressway|ja|日豊自動車道|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nippō Expressway|qq=}}. == References == * ''Photographic Collection: Komo Shrine, Shinmon Volume,'' Komo Culture Research Institute, 1999 * Kenichi Tanigawa (Ed.), ''Japanese Gods and Sacred Sites, Volume 1: Kyūshū,'' Hakusuisha, 1984, ISBN 4-560-02211-9 * ''Cultural Properties of Ōita Prefecture,'' Ōita Prefectural Board of Education, 1991 == See Also == * [[:en:Hata clan|Hata clan]] * {{ill|Daigo Hisa|ja|大悟法久志|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daigo Hisa|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://www.komojinja.jp/ Official Website of Komo Shrine] {{Shinto navbox}} {{translated page|ja|薦神社|version=101324642|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Shrines in Ōita Prefecture]] [[Category:Hachiman Shrines]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Shrines dedicated to Empress Jingū]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Historic Sites in Ōita Prefecture]] [[Category:Natural Monuments in Ōita Prefecture]] [[Category:Edo Period architecture]] [[Category:17th-century architecture]] [[Category:Nakatsu, Ōita]] }} [[sn:Komo Shrine (Oita)]] [[ja:薦神社]] [[Category:Buildings and structures completed in the 17th century]] [[Category:Buildings in Nakatsu, Oita]] [[Category:History of Nakatsu, Oita]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Oita Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ōita Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Oita prefecture]] [[Category:Designated historic sites of Oita Prefecture]] [[Category:大分県指定天然記念物]] [[Category:Edo period architecture]] [[Category:Cultural heritage monuments in Japan]]
