{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Komo Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Komo.jpg|280px|Main Gate]]
Shinmon (Sacred Gate) | A11ress = [[:en:Ōita Prefecture|Ōita Prefecture]], [[:en:Nakatsu, Ōita|Nakatsu, Ōita]], Ōaza Osada 209 | coordinates = {{coord|33|34|2.2|N|131|13|5.1|E|region:JP-44_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | Deities = [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]]
{{ill|Hime-no-Kami|ja|比売大神|lt=Hime-no-Ōkami|1=Himegami|3=比売神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hime-no-Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Himegami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 比売神}}
{{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|息長帯比売命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Jingū|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神功皇后}} | Status = Former [[Prefectural Shrine]] (県社) | Established = [[Shōwa era (Japan)|Shōwa era]] (9th century) | Main Hall = [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] | Alternate name = Ōsada Hachiman Shrine (大貞八幡宮) | Festivals = April 21 (festival) | Map = {{location map|Japan Oita|label=Komo Shrine|lat_deg=33|lat_min=34|lat_sec=2.2|lon_deg=131|lon_min=13|lon_sec=5.1|position=top|width=210|float=center|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=210|coord={{coord|33|34|2.2|N|131|13|5.1|E}}}} }} '''Komo Shrine''' (薦神社) is a [[:en:Hachiman shrine|Hachiman Shrine]] located in [[:en:Nakatsu, Ōita|Nakatsu, Ōita]], [[:en:Ōita Prefecture|Ōita Prefecture]]. It is also referred to as the Ōsada Hachiman Shrine (大貞八幡宮). The shrine features a sacred triangular pond, Misumi Pond (三角池, also historically written as 御澄池), representing the inner shrine, while the sacred hall is considered the outer shrine. This ancient shrine is said to be the progenitor of [[:en:Usa Jingū|Usa Shrine]], the primary shrine of all Hachiman shrines in Japan, with origins dating to the [[:en:Jōwa|Jōwa era]] (834–848). For this reason it is often called "Moto-Usa" == Enshrined Deities == * '''Main Hall''': ''' [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]] (Hachiman Ōkami) ''' {{ill|Hime-no-Kami|ja|比咩大神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hime-no-Kami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 比売神}} (The Three Go11esses of Munakata) ''' {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|息長帯比売命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Jingū|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神功皇后}} * '''Yasaka Shrine (Gion Shrine)''': ''' {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞嗚尊|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}} (Susanoo-no-Mikoto) * '''Wakamiya Shrine''': ''' Offspring of Emperor Ōjin Hime-no-Kami refers to the {{ill|Three Go11esses of Munakata|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=Three Munakata Go11esses|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Go11esses of Munakata|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=}}, represented by: * '''Tagorihime-no-Mikoto''' (沖津宮 at [[:en:Okinoshima (Fukuoka)|Okinoshima]]) * '''Tatsutsuhime-no-Mikoto''' (中津宮 at {{ill|Ōshima (Fukuoka)|ja|大島_(福岡県)|fa|اوشیما (استان فوکوئوکا)|ceb|Ō_Shima_(pulo_sa_Hapon,_Fukuoka-ken)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōshima (Fukuoka)|qq=}}) * '''Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto''' (辺津宮 at {{ill|Munakata Taisha|ja|宗像市田島|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata Taisha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}). According to the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], Volume 1, Kami Age, Chapter 6, Variation 3, the Three Go11esses were said to have descended at Usa Island in Ashihara-no-Nakatsukuni, marking their connection to this sacred site. == Shrine Crest == The shrine uses the "Single Tomoe" crest (一つ巴). Unlike the "Triple Tomoe" of Usa Shrine, the Single Tomoe indicates Komo Shrine's status as a progenitor shrine. Some scholars suggest that the shrine originally enshrined one of the Three Go11esses, possibly Tatsutsuhime-no-Mikoto, rather than all three. == History == In [[:en:Yōrō|Yōrō]] 4 (720), during the [[:en:Hayato rebellion|Hayato Rebellion]], the forces led by [[:en:Ōtomo no Tabito|Ōtomo no Tabito]] and Usa's "Divine Army," under Karashima Haduome, used sacred reed mats (薦枕, Komomakura) from Misumi Pond as a divine artifact to quell the rebellion. The Komokari Festival commemorates this event and is still performed every six years as part of the {{ill|Usa Shrine Procession|ja|宇佐神宮行幸会|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usa Shrine Procession|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} by the Karashima clan. Komo Shrine and Misumi Pond were designated as: * '''Ōita Prefectural Historic Site''' (March 30, 1976) * '''Ōita Prefectural Natural Monument''' for its aquatic and wetland plant communities. The Shinmon Gate, constructed by [[:en:Hosokawa Tadaoki|Hosokawa Tadaoki]] in [[:en:Yuanhe|Genna]] 8 (1622), is a [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]]. The shrine buildings date to the late Edo period. == Annual Rituals == * '''February 11''': Chinyoku Festival and Goshinkō-e (Demon Banishing Ceremony) * '''April 21''': festival * '''Third Saturday and Sunday in September''': Nakashū Festival (Sacred Procession) == Access == * From {{ill|Kokura Station|ja|小倉駅|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokura Station|qq=}}: Take the [[:en:Sonic|Sonic (train)]], a limited express train on the [[:en:Nippō Main Line|Nippō Main Line]], to [[:en:Nakatsu Station|Nakatsu Station]] (approximately 30 minutes). A 15-minute drive from the station. * By Bus: From Nakatsu Station South Exit, take a {{ill|Daiwa Kotsu|ja|大交北部バス|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daiwa Kotsu|qq=}} bound for Tanaka or Shōnen Gakuin-Kansha-mae and alight at "Komo Shrine" (approximately 20 minutes). * By Car: Approximately 15 minutes from the Nakatsu IC on the {{ill|Nippō Expressway|ja|日豊自動車道|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nippō Expressway|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. == References == * ''Photographic Collection: Komo Shrine, Shinmon Volume,'' Komo Culture Research Institute, 1999 * Kenichi Tanigawa (Ed.), ''Japanese Gods and Sacred Sites, Volume 1: Kyūshū,'' Hakusuisha, 1984, ISBN 4-560-02211-9 * ''Cultural Properties of Ōita Prefecture,'' Ōita Prefectural Board of Education, 1991 == See Also == * [[:en:Hata clan|Hata clan]] * {{ill|Daigo Hisa|ja|大悟法久志|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daigo Hisa|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://www.komojinja.jp/ Official Website of Komo Shrine] {{Shinto navbox}} {{translated page|ja|薦神社|version=101324642|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Shrines in Ōita Prefecture]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Shrines dedicated to Empress Jingū]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Historic Sites in Ōita Prefecture]] [[Category:Natural Monuments in Ōita Prefecture]] [[Category:17th-century architecture]] [[Category:Nakatsu, Ōita]] }} [[sn:Komo Shrine (Oita)]] [[ja:薦神社]] [[Category:Buildings and structures completed in the 17th century]] [[Category:Buildings in Nakatsu, Oita]] [[Category:History of Nakatsu, Oita]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Oita Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ōita Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Oita prefecture]] [[Category:Designated historic sites of Oita Prefecture]] [[Category:大分県指定天然記念物]] [[Category:Cultural heritage monuments in Japan]]

A 15-minute drive from the station. * By Bus: From Nakatsu Station South Exit, take a {{ill|Daiwa Kotsu|ja|大交北部バス|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daiwa Kotsu|qq=}} bound for Tanaka or Shōnen Gakuin-Kansha-mae and alight at "Komo Shrine" (approximately 20 minutes). * By Car: Approximately 15 minutes from the Nakatsu IC on the {{ill|Nippō Expressway|ja|日豊自動車道|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nippō Expressway|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. == References == * ''Photographic Collection: Komo Shrine, Shinmon Volume,'' Komo Culture Research Institute, 1999 * Kenichi Tanigawa (Ed.), ''Japanese Gods and Sacred Sites, Volume 1: Kyūshū,'' Hakusuisha, 1984, ISBN 4-560-02211-9 * ''Cultural Properties of Ōita Prefecture,'' Ōita Prefectural Board of Education, 1991 == See Also == * [[:en:Hata clan|Hata clan]] * {{ill|Daigo Hisa|ja|大悟法久志|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daigo Hisa|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://www.komojinja.jp/ Official Website of Komo Shrine] {{Shinto navbox}} {{translated page|ja|薦神社|version=101324642|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Shrines in Ōita Prefecture]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Shrines dedicated to Empress Jingū]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:17th-century architecture]] [[Category:Nakatsu, Ōita]] }} [[sn:Komo Shrine (Oita)]] [[ja:薦神社]] [[Category:Buildings and structures completed in the 17th century]] [[Category:Buildings in Nakatsu, Oita]] [[Category:History of Nakatsu, Oita]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Oita Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ōita Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Oita prefecture]] [[Category:Designated historic sites of Oita Prefecture]] [[Category:大分県指定天然記念物]] [[Category:Cultural heritage monuments in Japan]]

revision.comment: Bot: remove deleted category Shrines in Ōita Prefecture
revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Komo Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Komo.jpg|280px|Main Gate]]
Shinmon (Sacred Gate) | A11ress = [[:en:Ōita Prefecture|Ōita Prefecture]], [[:en:Nakatsu, Ōita|Nakatsu, Ōita]], Ōaza Osada 209 | coordinates = {{coord|33|34|2.2|N|131|13|5.1|E|region:JP-44_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | Deities = [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]]
{{ill|Hime-no-Kami|ja|比売大神|lt=Hime-no-Ōkami|1=Himegami|3=比売神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hime-no-Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Himegami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 比売神}}
{{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|息長帯比売命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Jingū|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神功皇后}} | Status = Former [[Prefectural Shrine]] (県社) | Established = [[Shōwa era (Japan)|Shōwa era]] (9th century) | Main Hall = [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] | Alternate name = Ōsada Hachiman Shrine (大貞八幡宮) | Festivals = April 21 (festival) | Map = {{location map|Japan Oita|label=Komo Shrine|lat_deg=33|lat_min=34|lat_sec=2.2|lon_deg=131|lon_min=13|lon_sec=5.1|position=top|width=210|float=center|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=210|coord={{coord|33|34|2.2|N|131|13|5.1|E}}}} }} '''Komo Shrine''' (薦神社) is a [[:en:Hachiman shrine|Hachiman Shrine]] located in [[:en:Nakatsu, Ōita|Nakatsu, Ōita]], [[:en:Ōita Prefecture|Ōita Prefecture]]. It is also referred to as the Ōsada Hachiman Shrine (大貞八幡宮). The shrine features a sacred triangular pond, Misumi Pond (三角池, also historically written as 御澄池), representing the inner shrine, while the sacred hall is considered the outer shrine. This ancient shrine is said to be the progenitor of [[:en:Usa Jingū|Usa Shrine]], the primary shrine of all Hachiman shrines in Japan, with origins dating to the [[:en:Jōwa|Jōwa era]] (834–848). For this reason it is often called "Moto-Usa" == Enshrined Deities == * '''Main Hall''': ''' [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]] (Hachiman Ōkami) ''' {{ill|Hime-no-Kami|ja|比咩大神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hime-no-Kami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 比売神}} (The Three Go11esses of Munakata) ''' {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|息長帯比売命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Jingū|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神功皇后}} * '''Yasaka Shrine (Gion Shrine)''': ''' {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞嗚尊|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}} (Susanoo-no-Mikoto) * '''Wakamiya Shrine''': ''' Offspring of Emperor Ōjin Hime-no-Kami refers to the {{ill|Three Go11esses of Munakata|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=Three Munakata Go11esses|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Go11esses of Munakata|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=}}, represented by: * '''Tagorihime-no-Mikoto''' (沖津宮 at [[:en:Okinoshima (Fukuoka)|Okinoshima]]) * '''Tatsutsuhime-no-Mikoto''' (中津宮 at {{ill|Ōshima (Fukuoka)|ja|大島_(福岡県)|fa|اوشیما (استان فوکوئوکا)|ceb|Ō_Shima_(pulo_sa_Hapon,_Fukuoka-ken)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōshima (Fukuoka)|qq=}}) * '''Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto''' (辺津宮 at {{ill|Munakata Taisha|ja|宗像市田島|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata Taisha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}). According to the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], Volume 1, Kami Age, Chapter 6, Variation 3, the Three Go11esses were said to have descended at Usa Island in Ashihara-no-Nakatsukuni, marking their connection to this sacred site. == Shrine Crest == The shrine uses the "Single Tomoe" crest (一つ巴). Unlike the "Triple Tomoe" of Usa Shrine, the Single Tomoe indicates Komo Shrine's status as a progenitor shrine. Some scholars suggest that the shrine originally enshrined one of the Three Go11esses, possibly Tatsutsuhime-no-Mikoto, rather than all three. == History == In [[:en:Yōrō|Yōrō]] 4 (720), during the [[:en:Hayato rebellion|Hayato Rebellion]], the forces led by [[:en:Ōtomo no Tabito|Ōtomo no Tabito]] and Usa's "Divine Army," under Karashima Haduome, used sacred reed mats (薦枕, Komomakura) from Misumi Pond as a divine artifact to quell the rebellion. The Komokari Festival commemorates this event and is still performed every six years as part of the {{ill|Usa Shrine Procession|ja|宇佐神宮行幸会|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usa Shrine Procession|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} by the Karashima clan. Komo Shrine and Misumi Pond were designated as: * '''Ōita Prefectural Historic Site''' (March 30, 1976) * '''Ōita Prefectural Natural Monument''' for its aquatic and wetland plant communities. The Shinmon Gate, constructed by [[:en:Hosokawa Tadaoki|Hosokawa Tadaoki]] in [[:en:Yuanhe|Genna]] 8 (1622), is a [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]]. The shrine buildings date to the late Edo period. == Annual Rituals == * '''February 11''': Chinyoku Festival and Goshinkō-e (Demon Banishing Ceremony) * '''April 21''': festival * '''Third Saturday and Sunday in September''': Nakashū Festival (Sacred Procession) == Access == * From {{ill|Kokura Station|ja|小倉駅|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokura Station|qq=}}: Take the [[:en:Sonic|Sonic (train)]], a limited express train on the [[:en:Nippō Main Line|Nippō Main Line]], to [[:en:Nakatsu Station|Nakatsu Station]] (approximately 30 minutes). A 15-minute drive from the station. * By Bus: From Nakatsu Station South Exit, take a {{ill|Daiwa Kotsu|ja|大交北部バス|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daiwa Kotsu|qq=}} bound for Tanaka or Shōnen Gakuin-Kansha-mae and alight at "Komo Shrine" (approximately 20 minutes). * By Car: Approximately 15 minutes from the Nakatsu IC on the {{ill|Nippō Expressway|ja|日豊自動車道|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nippō Expressway|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. == References == * ''Photographic Collection: Komo Shrine, Shinmon Volume,'' Komo Culture Research Institute, 1999 * Kenichi Tanigawa (Ed.), ''Japanese Gods and Sacred Sites, Volume 1: Kyūshū,'' Hakusuisha, 1984, ISBN 4-560-02211-9 * ''Cultural Properties of Ōita Prefecture,'' Ōita Prefectural Board of Education, 1991 == See Also == * [[:en:Hata clan|Hata clan]] * {{ill|Daigo Hisa|ja|大悟法久志|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daigo Hisa|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://www.komojinja.jp/ Official Website of Komo Shrine] {{Shinto navbox}} {{translated page|ja|薦神社|version=101324642|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Shrines dedicated to Empress Jingū]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:17th-century architecture]] [[Category:Nakatsu, Ōita]] }} [[sn:Komo Shrine (Oita)]] [[ja:薦神社]] [[Category:Buildings and structures completed in the 17th century]] [[Category:Buildings in Nakatsu, Oita]] [[Category:History of Nakatsu, Oita]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Oita Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ōita Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Oita prefecture]] [[Category:Designated historic sites of Oita Prefecture]] [[Category:大分県指定天然記念物]] [[Category:Cultural heritage monuments in Japan]]

According to the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], Volume 1, Kami Age, Chapter 6, Variation 3, the Three Go11esses were said to have descended at Usa Island in Ashihara-no-Nakatsukuni, marking their connection to this sacred site. == Shrine Crest == The shrine uses the "Single Tomoe" crest (一つ巴). Unlike the "Triple Tomoe" of Usa Shrine, the Single Tomoe indicates Komo Shrine's status as a progenitor shrine. Some scholars suggest that the shrine originally enshrined one of the Three Go11esses, possibly Tatsutsuhime-no-Mikoto, rather than all three. == History == In [[:en:Yōrō|Yōrō]] 4 (720), during the [[:en:Hayato rebellion|Hayato Rebellion]], the forces led by [[:en:Ōtomo no Tabito|Ōtomo no Tabito]] and Usa's "Divine Army," under Karashima Haduome, used sacred reed mats (薦枕, Komomakura) from Misumi Pond as a divine artifact to quell the rebellion. The Komokari Festival commemorates this event and is still performed every six years as part of the {{ill|Usa Shrine Procession|ja|宇佐神宮行幸会|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usa Shrine Procession|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} by the Karashima clan. Komo Shrine and Misumi Pond were designated as: * '''Ōita Prefectural Historic Site''' (March 30, 1976) * '''Ōita Prefectural Natural Monument''' for its aquatic and wetland plant communities. The Shinmon Gate, constructed by [[:en:Hosokawa Tadaoki|Hosokawa Tadaoki]] in [[:en:Yuanhe|Genna]] 8 (1622), is a [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]]. The shrine buildings date to the late Edo period. == Annual Rituals == * '''February 11''': Chinyoku Festival and Goshinkō-e (Demon Banishing Ceremony) * '''April 21''': festival * '''Third Saturday and Sunday in September''': Nakashū Festival (Sacred Procession) == Access == * From {{ill|Kokura Station|ja|小倉駅|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokura Station|qq=}}: Take the [[:en:Sonic|Sonic (train)]], a limited express train on the [[:en:Nippō Main Line|Nippō Main Line]], to [[:en:Nakatsu Station|Nakatsu Station]] (approximately 30 minutes). A 15-minute drive from the station. * By Bus: From Nakatsu Station South Exit, take a {{ill|Daiwa Kotsu|ja|大交北部バス|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daiwa Kotsu|qq=}} bound for Tanaka or Shōnen Gakuin-Kansha-mae and alight at "Komo Shrine" (approximately 20 minutes). * By Car: Approximately 15 minutes from the Nakatsu IC on the {{ill|Nippō Expressway|ja|日豊自動車道|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nippō Expressway|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. == References == * ''Photographic Collection: Komo Shrine, Shinmon Volume,'' Komo Culture Research Institute, 1999 * Kenichi Tanigawa (Ed.), ''Japanese Gods and Sacred Sites, Volume 1: Kyūshū,'' Hakusuisha, 1984, ISBN 4-560-02211-9 * ''Cultural Properties of Ōita Prefecture,'' Ōita Prefectural Board of Education, 1991 == See Also == * [[:en:Hata clan|Hata clan]] * {{ill|Daigo Hisa|ja|大悟法久志|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daigo Hisa|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://www.komojinja.jp/ Official Website of Komo Shrine] {{Shinto navbox}} {{translated page|ja|薦神社|version=101324642|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Shrines dedicated to Empress Jingū]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:17th-century architecture]] [[Category:Nakatsu, Ōita]] }} [[sn:Komo Shrine (Oita)]] [[ja:薦神社]] [[Category:Buildings and structures completed in the 17th century]] [[Category:Buildings in Nakatsu, Oita]] [[Category:History of Nakatsu, Oita]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Oita Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ōita Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Oita prefecture]] [[Category:Designated historic sites of Oita Prefecture]] [[Category:Natural Monuments Designated in Oita Prefecture]] [[Category:Cultural heritage monuments in Japan]]

It is also referred to as the Ōsada Hachiman Shrine (大貞八幡宮). The shrine features a sacred triangular pond, Misumi Pond (三角池, also historically written as 御澄池), representing the inner shrine, while the sacred hall is considered the outer shrine. This ancient shrine is said to be the progenitor of [[:en:Usa Jingū|Usa Shrine]], the primary shrine of all Hachiman shrines in Japan, with origins dating to the [[:en:Jōwa|Jōwa era]] (834–848). For this reason it is often called "Moto-Usa" == Enshrined Deities == * '''Main Hall''': ''' [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]] (Hachiman Ōkami) ''' {{ill|Hime-no-Kami|ja|比咩大神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hime-no-Kami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 比売神}} (The Three Go11esses of Munakata) ''' {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|息長帯比売命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Jingū|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神功皇后}} * '''Yasaka Shrine (Gion Shrine)''': ''' {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞嗚尊|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}} (Susanoo-no-Mikoto) * '''Wakamiya Shrine''': ''' Offspring of Emperor Ōjin Hime-no-Kami refers to the {{ill|Three Go11esses of Munakata|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=Three Munakata Go11esses|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Go11esses of Munakata|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=}}, represented by: * '''Tagorihime-no-Mikoto''' (沖津宮 at [[:en:Okinoshima (Fukuoka)|Okinoshima]]) * '''Tatsutsuhime-no-Mikoto''' (中津宮 at {{ill|Ōshima (Fukuoka)|ja|大島_(福岡県)|fa|اوشیما (استان فوکوئوکا)|ceb|Ō_Shima_(pulo_sa_Hapon,_Fukuoka-ken)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōshima (Fukuoka)|qq=}}) * '''Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto''' (辺津宮 at {{ill|Munakata Taisha|ja|宗像市田島|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata Taisha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}). According to the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], Volume 1, Kami Age, Chapter 6, Variation 3, the Three Go11esses were said to have descended at Usa Island in Ashihara-no-Nakatsukuni, marking their connection to this sacred site. == Shrine Crest == The shrine uses the "Single Tomoe" crest (一つ巴). The Komokari Festival commemorates this event and is still performed every six years as part of the {{ill|Usa Shrine Procession|ja|宇佐神宮行幸会|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usa Shrine Procession|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} by the Karashima clan. The shrine features a sacred triangular pond, Misumi Pond (三角池, also historically written as 御澄池), representing the inner shrine, while the sacred hall is considered the outer shrine. This ancient shrine is said to be the progenitor of [[:en:Usa Jingū|Usa Shrine]], the primary shrine of all Hachiman shrines in Japan, with origins dating to the [[:en:Jōwa|Jōwa era]] (834–848). For this reason it is often called "Moto-Usa" == Enshrined Deities == * '''Main Hall''': ''' [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]] (Hachiman Ōkami) ''' {{ill|Hime-no-Kami|ja|比咩大神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hime-no-Kami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 比売神|1=Himegami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q22070227}} (The Three Go11esses of Munakata) ''' {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|息長帯比売命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Jingū|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神功皇后|1=Empress Jingū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q232803}} * '''Yasaka Shrine (Gion Shrine)''': ''' {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞嗚尊|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}} (Susanoo-no-Mikoto) * '''Wakamiya Shrine''': ''' Offspring of Emperor Ōjin Hime-no-Kami refers to the {{ill|Three Go11esses of Munakata|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=Three Munakata Go11esses|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Go11esses of Munakata|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=}}, represented by: * '''Tagorihime-no-Mikoto''' (沖津宮 at [[:en:Okinoshima (Fukuoka)|Okinoshima]]) * '''Tatsutsuhime-no-Mikoto''' (中津宮 at {{ill|Ōshima (Fukuoka)|ja|大島_(福岡県)|fa|اوشیما (استان فوکوئوکا)|ceb|Ō_Shima_(pulo_sa_Hapon,_Fukuoka-ken)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōshima (Fukuoka)|qq=}}) * '''Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto''' (辺津宮 at {{ill|Munakata Taisha|ja|宗像市田島|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata Taisha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}). According to the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], Volume 1, Kami Age, Chapter 6, Variation 3, the Three Go11esses were said to have descended at Usa Island in Ashihara-no-Nakatsukuni, marking their connection to this sacred site. == Shrine Crest == The shrine uses the "Single Tomoe" crest (一つ巴). Unlike the "Triple Tomoe" of Usa Shrine, the Single Tomoe indicates Komo Shrine's status as a progenitor shrine. Some scholars suggest that the shrine originally enshrined one of the Three Go11esses, possibly Tatsutsuhime-no-Mikoto, rather than all three. == History == In [[:en:Yōrō|Yōrō]] 4 (720), during the [[:en:Hayato rebellion|Hayato Rebellion]], the forces led by [[:en:Ōtomo no Tabito|Ōtomo no Tabito]] and Usa's "Divine Army," under Karashima Haduome, used sacred reed mats (薦枕, Komomakura) from Misumi Pond as a divine artifact to quell the rebellion. The Komokari Festival commemorates this event and is still performed every six years as part of the {{ill|Usa Shrine Procession|ja|宇佐神宮行幸会|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usa Shrine Procession|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} by the Karashima clan. Komo Shrine and Misumi Pond were designated as: * '''Ōita Prefectural Historic Site''' (March 30, 1976) * '''Ōita Prefectural Natural Monument''' for its aquatic and wetland plant communities. The Shinmon Gate, constructed by [[:en:Hosokawa Tadaoki|Hosokawa Tadaoki]] in [[:en:Yuanhe|Genna]] 8 (1622), is a [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]]. The shrine buildings date to the late Edo period. == Annual Rituals == * '''February 11''': Chinyoku Festival and Goshinkō-e (Demon Banishing Ceremony) * '''April 21''': festival * '''Third Saturday and Sunday in September''': Nakashū Festival (Sacred Procession) == Access == * From {{ill|Kokura Station|ja|小倉駅|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokura Station|qq=|1=Kokura Station|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10957775}}: Take the [[:en:Sonic|Sonic (train)]], a limited express train on the [[:en:Nippō Main Line|Nippō Main Line]], to [[:en:Nakatsu Station|Nakatsu Station]] (approximately 30 minutes). A 15-minute drive from the station. * By Bus: From Nakatsu Station South Exit, take a {{ill|Daiwa Kotsu|ja|大交北部バス|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daiwa Kotsu|qq=|1=Daiko Hokubu Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432248}} bound for Tanaka or Shōnen Gakuin-Kansha-mae and alight at "Komo Shrine" (approximately 20 minutes). * By Car: Approximately 15 minutes from the Nakatsu IC on the {{ill|Nippō Expressway|ja|日豊自動車道|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nippō Expressway|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. == References == * ''Photographic Collection: Komo Shrine, Shinmon Volume,'' Komo Culture Research Institute, 1999 * Kenichi Tanigawa (Ed.), ''Japanese Gods and Sacred Sites, Volume 1: Kyūshū,'' Hakusuisha, 1984, ISBN 4-560-02211-9 * ''Cultural Properties of Ōita Prefecture,'' Ōita Prefectural Board of Education, 1991 == See Also == * [[:en:Hata clan|Hata clan]] * {{ill|Daigo Hisa|ja|大悟法久志|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daigo Hisa|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://www.komojinja.jp/ Official Website of Komo Shrine] {{Shinto navbox}} {{translated page|ja|薦神社|version=101324642|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Shrines dedicated to Empress Jingū]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:17th-century architecture]] [[Category:Nakatsu, Ōita]] }} [[sn:Komo Shrine (Oita)]] [[ja:薦神社]] [[Category:Buildings and structures completed in the 17th century]] [[Category:Buildings in Nakatsu, Oita]] [[Category:History of Nakatsu, Oita]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Oita Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ōita Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Oita prefecture]] [[Category:Designated historic sites of Oita Prefecture]] [[Category:Natural Monuments Designated in Oita Prefecture]] [[Category:Cultural heritage monuments in Japan]]

[[ja:薦神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11622260]] Wikidata name: Komo-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:37, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11622260]] Wikidata name: Komo-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:16, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Komo Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Komo.jpg | A11ress = [[:en:Ōita Prefecture|Ōita Prefecture]], [[:en:Nakatsu, Ōita|Nakatsu, Ōita]], Ōaza Osada 209 | coordinates = {{Coord|33.567278|N|131.218139|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | Deities = [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]]
{{ill|Hime-no-Kami|ja|比売大神|lt=Hime-no-Ōkami|1=Himegami|3=比売神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hime-no-Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Himegami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 比売神}}
{{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|息長帯比売命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Jingū|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神功皇后|1=Empress Jingū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q232803}} | Status = Former [[Prefectural Shrine]] (県社) | Established = [[Shōwa era (Japan)|Shōwa era]] (9th century) | Main Hall = [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] | Alternate name = Ōsada Hachiman Shrine (大貞八幡宮) | Festivals = April 21 (festival) | Map = {{location map|Japan Oita|label=Komo Shrine|lat_deg=33|lat_min=34|lat_sec=2.2|lon_deg=131|lon_min=13|lon_sec=5.1|position=top|width=210|float=center|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=210|coord={{coord|33|34|2.2|N|131|13|5.1|E}}}} | prefecture= {{ill|Nakatsu|qid=Q861221}} | architecture_style= {{ill|nagare-zukuri|qid=Q210684}} }} '''Komo Shrine''' (薦神社) is a [[:en:Hachiman shrine|Hachiman Shrine]] located in [[:en:Nakatsu, Ōita|Nakatsu, Ōita]], [[:en:Ōita Prefecture|Ōita Prefecture]]. It is also referred to as the Ōsada Hachiman Shrine (大貞八幡宮). The shrine features a sacred triangular pond, Misumi Pond (三角池, also historically written as 御澄池), representing the inner shrine, while the sacred hall is considered the outer shrine. This ancient shrine is said to be the progenitor of [[:en:Usa Jingū|Usa Shrine]], the primary shrine of all Hachiman shrines in Japan, with origins dating to the [[:en:Jōwa|Jōwa era]] (834–848). For this reason it is often called "Moto-Usa" == Enshrined Deities == * '''Main Hall''': ''' [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]] (Hachiman Ōkami) ''' {{ill|Hime-no-Kami|ja|比咩大神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hime-no-Kami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 比売神|1=Himegami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q22070227}} (The Three Go11esses of Munakata) ''' {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|息長帯比売命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Jingū|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神功皇后|1=Empress Jingū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q232803}} * '''Yasaka Shrine (Gion Shrine)''': ''' {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞嗚尊|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}} (Susanoo-no-Mikoto) * '''Wakamiya Shrine''': ''' Offspring of Emperor Ōjin Hime-no-Kami refers to the {{ill|Three Go11esses of Munakata|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=Three Munakata Go11esses|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Go11esses of Munakata|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=}}, represented by: * '''Tagorihime-no-Mikoto''' (沖津宮 at [[:en:Okinoshima (Fukuoka)|Okinoshima]]) * '''Tatsutsuhime-no-Mikoto''' (中津宮 at {{ill|Ōshima (Fukuoka)|ja|大島_(福岡県)|fa|اوشیما (استان فوکوئوکا)|ceb|Ō_Shima_(pulo_sa_Hapon,_Fukuoka-ken)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōshima (Fukuoka)|qq=}}) * '''Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto''' (辺津宮 at {{ill|Munakata Taisha|ja|宗像市田島|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata Taisha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}). According to the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], Volume 1, Kami Age, Chapter 6, Variation 3, the Three Go11esses were said to have descended at Usa Island in Ashihara-no-Nakatsukuni, marking their connection to this sacred site. == Shrine Crest == The shrine uses the "Single Tomoe" crest (一つ巴). Unlike the "Triple Tomoe" of Usa Shrine, the Single Tomoe indicates Komo Shrine's status as a progenitor shrine. Some scholars suggest that the shrine originally enshrined one of the Three Go11esses, possibly Tatsutsuhime-no-Mikoto, rather than all three. == History == In [[:en:Yōrō|Yōrō]] 4 (720), during the [[:en:Hayato rebellion|Hayato Rebellion]], the forces led by [[:en:Ōtomo no Tabito|Ōtomo no Tabito]] and Usa's "Divine Army," under Karashima Haduome, used sacred reed mats (薦枕, Komomakura) from Misumi Pond as a divine artifact to quell the rebellion. The Komokari Festival commemorates this event and is still performed every six years as part of the {{ill|Usa Shrine Procession|ja|宇佐神宮行幸会|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usa Shrine Procession|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} by the Karashima clan. Komo Shrine and Misumi Pond were designated as: * '''Ōita Prefectural Historic Site''' (March 30, 1976) * '''Ōita Prefectural Natural Monument''' for its aquatic and wetland plant communities. The Shinmon Gate, constructed by [[:en:Hosokawa Tadaoki|Hosokawa Tadaoki]] in [[:en:Yuanhe|Genna]] 8 (1622), is a [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]]. The shrine buildings date to the late Edo period. == Annual Rituals == * '''February 11''': Chinyoku Festival and Goshinkō-e (Demon Banishing Ceremony) * '''April 21''': festival * '''Third Saturday and Sunday in September''': Nakashū Festival (Sacred Procession) == Access == * From {{ill|Kokura Station|ja|小倉駅|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokura Station|qq=|1=Kokura Station|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10957775}}: Take the [[:en:Sonic|Sonic (train)]], a limited express train on the [[:en:Nippō Main Line|Nippō Main Line]], to [[:en:Nakatsu Station|Nakatsu Station]] (approximately 30 minutes). A 15-minute drive from the station. * By Bus: From Nakatsu Station South Exit, take a {{ill|Daiwa Kotsu|ja|大交北部バス|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daiwa Kotsu|qq=|1=Daiko Hokubu Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432248}} bound for Tanaka or Shōnen Gakuin-Kansha-mae and alight at "Komo Shrine" (approximately 20 minutes). * By Car: Approximately 15 minutes from the Nakatsu IC on the {{ill|Nippō Expressway|ja|日豊自動車道|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nippō Expressway|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. == References == * ''Photographic Collection: Komo Shrine, Shinmon Volume,'' Komo Culture Research Institute, 1999 * Kenichi Tanigawa (Ed.), ''Japanese Gods and Sacred Sites, Volume 1: Kyūshū,'' Hakusuisha, 1984, ISBN 4-560-02211-9 * ''Cultural Properties of Ōita Prefecture,'' Ōita Prefectural Board of Education, 1991 == See Also == * [[:en:Hata clan|Hata clan]] * {{ill|Daigo Hisa|ja|大悟法久志|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daigo Hisa|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://www.komojinja.jp/ Official Website of Komo Shrine] {{Shinto navbox}} {{translated page|ja|薦神社|version=101324642|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Shrines dedicated to Empress Jingū]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:17th-century architecture]] [[Category:Nakatsu, Ōita]] }} [[ja:薦神社]] [[Category:Buildings and structures completed in the 17th century]] [[Category:Buildings in Nakatsu, Oita]] [[Category:History of Nakatsu, Oita]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Oita Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ōita Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Oita prefecture]] [[Category:Designated historic sites of Oita Prefecture]] [[Category:Natural Monuments Designated in Oita Prefecture]] [[Category:Cultural heritage monuments in Japan]] {{wikidata link|Q11622260}}

The shrine features a sacred triangular pond, Misumi Pond (三角池, also historically written as 御澄池), representing the inner shrine, while the sacred hall is considered the outer shrine. This ancient shrine is said to be the progenitor of [[:en:Usa Jingū|Usa Shrine]], the primary shrine of all Hachiman shrines in Japan, with origins dating to the [[:en:Jōwa|Jōwa era]] (834–848). For this reason it is often called "Moto-Usa" == Enshrined Deities == * '''Main Hall''': ''' [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]] (Hachiman Ōkami) ''' {{ill|Hime-no-Kami|ja|比咩大神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hime-no-Kami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 比売神|1=Himegami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q22070227}} (The Three Go11esses of Munakata) ''' {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|息長帯比売命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Jingū|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神功皇后|1=Empress Jingū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q232803}} * '''Yasaka Shrine (Gion Shrine)''': ''' {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞嗚尊|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}} (Susanoo-no-Mikoto) * '''Wakamiya Shrine''': ''' Offspring of Emperor Ōjin Hime-no-Kami refers to the {{ill|Three Go11esses of Munakata|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=Three Munakata Go11esses|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Go11esses of Munakata|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=}}, represented by: * '''Tagorihime-no-Mikoto''' (沖津宮 at [[:en:Okinoshima (Fukuoka)|Okinoshima]]) * '''Tatsutsuhime-no-Mikoto''' (中津宮 at {{ill|Ōshima (Fukuoka)|ja|大島_(福岡県)|fa|اوشیما (استان فوکوئوکا)|ceb|Ō_Shima_(pulo_sa_Hapon,_Fukuoka-ken)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōshima (Fukuoka)|qq=}}) * '''Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto''' (辺津宮 at {{ill|Munakata Taisha|ja|宗像市田島|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata Taisha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}). According to the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], Volume 1, Kami Age, Chapter 6, Variation 3, the Three Go11esses were said to have descended at Usa Island in Ashihara-no-Nakatsukuni, marking their connection to this sacred site. == Shrine Crest == The shrine uses the "Single Tomoe" crest (一つ巴). Unlike the "Triple Tomoe" of Usa Shrine, the Single Tomoe indicates Komo Shrine's status as a progenitor shrine. Some scholars suggest that the shrine originally enshrined one of the Three Go11esses, possibly Tatsutsuhime-no-Mikoto, rather than all three. == History == In [[:en:Yōrō|Yōrō]] 4 (720), during the [[:en:Hayato rebellion|Hayato Rebellion]], the forces led by [[:en:Ōtomo no Tabito|Ōtomo no Tabito]] and Usa's "Divine Army," under Karashima Haduome, used sacred reed mats (薦枕, Komomakura) from Misumi Pond as a divine artifact to quell the rebellion. The Komokari Festival commemorates this event and is still performed every six years as part of the {{ill|Usa Shrine Procession|ja|宇佐神宮行幸会|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usa Shrine Procession|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} by the Karashima clan. Komo Shrine and Misumi Pond were designated as: * '''Ōita Prefectural Historic Site''' (March 30, 1976) * '''Ōita Prefectural Natural Monument''' for its aquatic and wetland plant communities. The Shinmon Gate, constructed by [[:en:Hosokawa Tadaoki|Hosokawa Tadaoki]] in [[:en:Yuanhe|Genna]] 8 (1622), is a [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]]. The shrine buildings date to the late Edo period. == Annual Rituals == * '''February 11''': Chinyoku Festival and Goshinkō-e (Demon Banishing Ceremony) * '''April 21''': festival * '''Third Saturday and Sunday in September''': Nakashū Festival (Sacred Procession) == Access == * From {{ill|Kokura Station|ja|小倉駅|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokura Station|qq=|1=Kokura Station|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10957775}}: Take the [[:en:Sonic|Sonic (train)]], a limited express train on the [[:en:Nippō Main Line|Nippō Main Line]], to [[:en:Nakatsu Station|Nakatsu Station]] (approximately 30 minutes). A 15-minute drive from the station. * By Bus: From Nakatsu Station South Exit, take a {{ill|Daiwa Kotsu|ja|大交北部バス|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daiwa Kotsu|qq=|1=Daiko Hokubu Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432248}} bound for Tanaka or Shōnen Gakuin-Kansha-mae and alight at "Komo Shrine" (approximately 20 minutes). * By Car: Approximately 15 minutes from the Nakatsu IC on the {{ill|Nippō Expressway|ja|日豊自動車道|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nippō Expressway|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. == References == * ''Photographic Collection: Komo Shrine, Shinmon Volume,'' Komo Culture Research Institute, 1999 * Kenichi Tanigawa (Ed.), ''Japanese Gods and Sacred Sites, Volume 1: Kyūshū,'' Hakusuisha, 1984, ISBN 4-560-02211-9 * ''Cultural Properties of Ōita Prefecture,'' Ōita Prefectural Board of Education, 1991 == See Also == * [[:en:Hata clan|Hata clan]] * {{ill|Daigo Hisa|ja|大悟法久志|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daigo Hisa|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://www.komojinja.jp/ Official Website of Komo Shrine] {{Shinto navbox}} {{translated page|ja|薦神社|version=101324642|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Shrines dedicated to Empress Jingū]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:17th-century architecture]] [[Category:Nakatsu, Ōita]] }} [[ja:薦神社]] [[Category:Buildings and structures completed in the 17th century]] [[Category:Buildings in Nakatsu, Oita]] [[Category:History of Nakatsu, Oita]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Oita Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ōita Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Oita prefecture]] [[Category:Designated historic sites of Oita Prefecture]] [[Category:Natural Monuments Designated in Oita Prefecture]] [[Category:Cultural heritage monuments in Japan]] {{wikidata link|Q11622260}}

revision.text:
[[ja:薦神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11622260]] Wikidata name: Komo-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:37, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11622260]] Wikidata name: Komo-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:16, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Komo Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Komo.jpg | A11ress = [[:en:Ōita Prefecture|Ōita Prefecture]], [[:en:Nakatsu, Ōita|Nakatsu, Ōita]], Ōaza Osada 209 | coordinates = {{Coord|33.567278|N|131.218139|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | Deities = [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]]
{{ill|Hime-no-Kami|ja|比売大神|lt=Hime-no-Ōkami|1=Himegami|3=比売神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hime-no-Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Himegami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 比売神}}
{{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|息長帯比売命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Jingū|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神功皇后|1=Empress Jingū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q232803}} | Status = Former [[Prefectural Shrine]] (県社) | Established = [[Shōwa era (Japan)|Shōwa era]] (9th century) | Main Hall = [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] | Alternate name = Ōsada Hachiman Shrine (大貞八幡宮) | Festivals = April 21 (festival) | Map = {{location map|Japan Oita|label=Komo Shrine|lat_deg=33|lat_min=34|lat_sec=2.2|lon_deg=131|lon_min=13|lon_sec=5.1|position=top|width=210|float=center|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=210|coord={{coord|33|34|2.2|N|131|13|5.1|E}}}} | prefecture= {{ill|Nakatsu|WD=Q861221}} | architecture_style= {{ill|nagare-zukuri|WD=Q210684}} }} '''Komo Shrine''' (薦神社) is a [[:en:Hachiman shrine|Hachiman Shrine]] located in [[:en:Nakatsu, Ōita|Nakatsu, Ōita]], [[:en:Ōita Prefecture|Ōita Prefecture]]. It is also referred to as the Ōsada Hachiman Shrine (大貞八幡宮). The shrine features a sacred triangular pond, Misumi Pond (三角池, also historically written as 御澄池), representing the inner shrine, while the sacred hall is considered the outer shrine. This ancient shrine is said to be the progenitor of [[:en:Usa Jingū|Usa Shrine]], the primary shrine of all Hachiman shrines in Japan, with origins dating to the [[:en:Jōwa|Jōwa era]] (834–848). For this reason it is often called "Moto-Usa" == Enshrined Deities == * '''Main Hall''': ''' [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]] (Hachiman Ōkami) ''' {{ill|Hime-no-Kami|ja|比咩大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hime-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 比売神|1=Himegami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q22070227}} (The Three Go11esses of Munakata) ''' {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|息長帯比売命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Jingū|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神功皇后|1=Empress Jingū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q232803}} * '''Yasaka Shrine (Gion Shrine)''': ''' {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞嗚尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}} (Susanoo-no-Mikoto) * '''Wakamiya Shrine''': ''' Offspring of Emperor Ōjin Hime-no-Kami refers to the {{ill|Three Go11esses of Munakata|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=Three Munakata Go11esses|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Go11esses of Munakata|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=}}, represented by: * '''Tagorihime-no-Mikoto''' (沖津宮 at [[:en:Okinoshima (Fukuoka)|Okinoshima]]) * '''Tatsutsuhime-no-Mikoto''' (中津宮 at {{ill|Ōshima (Fukuoka)|ja|大島_(福岡県)|fa|اوشیما (استان فوکوئوکا)|ceb|Ō_Shima_(pulo_sa_Hapon,_Fukuoka-ken)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōshima (Fukuoka)|qq=}}) * '''Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto''' (辺津宮 at {{ill|Munakata Taisha|ja|宗像市田島|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata Taisha|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}). According to the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], Volume 1, Kami Age, Chapter 6, Variation 3, the Three Go11esses were said to have descended at Usa Island in Ashihara-no-Nakatsukuni, marking their connection to this sacred site. == Shrine Crest == The shrine uses the "Single Tomoe" crest (一つ巴). Unlike the "Triple Tomoe" of Usa Shrine, the Single Tomoe indicates Komo Shrine's status as a progenitor shrine. Some scholars suggest that the shrine originally enshrined one of the Three Go11esses, possibly Tatsutsuhime-no-Mikoto, rather than all three. == History == In [[:en:Yōrō|Yōrō]] 4 (720), during the [[:en:Hayato rebellion|Hayato Rebellion]], the forces led by [[:en:Ōtomo no Tabito|Ōtomo no Tabito]] and Usa's "Divine Army," under Karashima Haduome, used sacred reed mats (薦枕, Komomakura) from Misumi Pond as a divine artifact to quell the rebellion. The Komokari Festival commemorates this event and is still performed every six years as part of the {{ill|Usa Shrine Procession|ja|宇佐神宮行幸会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usa Shrine Procession|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} by the Karashima clan. Komo Shrine and Misumi Pond were designated as: * '''Ōita Prefectural Historic Site''' (March 30, 1976) * '''Ōita Prefectural Natural Monument''' for its aquatic and wetland plant communities. The Shinmon Gate, constructed by [[:en:Hosokawa Tadaoki|Hosokawa Tadaoki]] in [[:en:Yuanhe|Genna]] 8 (1622), is a [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]]. The shrine buildings date to the late Edo period. == Annual Rituals == * '''February 11''': Chinyoku Festival and Goshinkō-e (Demon Banishing Ceremony) * '''April 21''': festival * '''Third Saturday and Sunday in September''': Nakashū Festival (Sacred Procession) == Access == * From {{ill|Kokura Station|ja|小倉駅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokura Station|1=Kokura Station|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10957775}}: Take the [[:en:Sonic|Sonic (train)]], a limited express train on the [[:en:Nippō Main Line|Nippō Main Line]], to [[:en:Nakatsu Station|Nakatsu Station]] (approximately 30 minutes). A 15-minute drive from the station. * By Bus: From Nakatsu Station South Exit, take a {{ill|Daiwa Kotsu|ja|大交北部バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daiwa Kotsu|1=Daiko Hokubu Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432248}} bound for Tanaka or Shōnen Gakuin-Kansha-mae and alight at "Komo Shrine" (approximately 20 minutes). * By Car: Approximately 15 minutes from the Nakatsu IC on the {{ill|Nippō Expressway|ja|日豊自動車道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nippō Expressway|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. == References == * ''Photographic Collection: Komo Shrine, Shinmon Volume,'' Komo Culture Research Institute, 1999 * Kenichi Tanigawa (Ed.), ''Japanese Gods and Sacred Sites, Volume 1: Kyūshū,'' Hakusuisha, 1984, ISBN 4-560-02211-9 * ''Cultural Properties of Ōita Prefecture,'' Ōita Prefectural Board of Education, 1991 == See Also == * [[:en:Hata clan|Hata clan]] * {{ill|Daigo Hisa|ja|大悟法久志|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daigo Hisa|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://www.komojinja.jp/ Official Website of Komo Shrine] {{Shinto navbox}} {{translated page|ja|薦神社|version=101324642|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Shrines dedicated to Empress Jingū]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:17th-century architecture]] [[Category:Nakatsu, Ōita]] }} [[ja:薦神社]] [[Category:Buildings and structures completed in the 17th century]] [[Category:Buildings in Nakatsu, Oita]] [[Category:History of Nakatsu, Oita]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Oita Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ōita Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Oita prefecture]] [[Category:Designated historic sites of Oita Prefecture]] [[Category:Natural Monuments Designated in Oita Prefecture]] [[Category:Cultural heritage monuments in Japan]] {{wikidata link|Q11622260}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

The shrine buildings date to the late Edo period. == Annual Rituals == * '''February 11''': Chinyoku Festival and Goshinkō-e (Demon Banishing Ceremony) * '''April 21''': festival * '''Third Saturday and Sunday in September''': Nakashū Festival (Sacred Procession) == Access == * From {{ill|Kokura Station|ja|小倉駅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokura Station|1=Kokura Station|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10957775}}: Take the [[:en:Sonic|Sonic (train)]], a limited express train on the [[:en:Nippō Main Line|Nippō Main Line]], to [[:en:Nakatsu Station|Nakatsu Station]] (approximately 30 minutes). A 15-minute drive from the station. * By Bus: From Nakatsu Station South Exit, take a {{ill|Daiwa Kotsu|ja|大交北部バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daiwa Kotsu|1=Daiko Hokubu Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432248}} bound for Tanaka or Shōnen Gakuin-Kansha-mae and alight at "Komo Shrine" (approximately 20 minutes). * By Car: Approximately 15 minutes from the Nakatsu IC on the {{ill|Nippō Expressway|ja|日豊自動車道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nippō Expressway|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. == References == * ''Photographic Collection: Komo Shrine, Shinmon Volume,'' Komo [[ja:薦神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11622260]] Wikidata name: Komo-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:37, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11622260]] Wikidata name: Komo-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:16, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Komo Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Komo.jpg | A11ress = [[:en:Ōita Prefecture|Ōita Prefecture]], [[:en:Nakatsu, Ōita|Nakatsu, Ōita]], Ōaza Osada 209 | coordinates = {{Coord|33.567278|N|131.218139|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | Deities = [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]]
{{ill|Hime-no-Kami|ja|比売大神|lt=Hime-no-Ōkami|1=Himegami|3=比売神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hime-no-Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Himegami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 比売神}}
{{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|息長帯比売命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Jingū|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神功皇后|1=Empress Jingū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q232803}} | Status = Former [[Prefectural Shrine]] (県社) | Established = [[Shōwa era (Japan)|Shōwa era]] (9th century) | Main Hall = [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] | Alternate name = Ōsada Hachiman Shrine (大貞八幡宮) | Festivals = April 21 (festival) | Map = {{location map|Japan Oita|label=Komo Shrine|lat_deg=33|lat_min=34|lat_sec=2.2|lon_deg=131|lon_min=13|lon_sec=5.1|position=top|width=210|float=center|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=210|coord={{coord|33|34|2.2|N|131|13|5.1|E}}}} | prefecture= {{ill|Nakatsu|WD=Q861221}} | architecture_style= {{ill|nagare-zukuri|WD=Q210684}} }} '''Komo Shrine''' (薦神社) is a [[:en:Hachiman shrine|Hachiman Shrine]] located in [[:en:Nakatsu, Ōita|Nakatsu, Ōita]], [[:en:Ōita Prefecture|Ōita Prefecture]]. It is also referred to as the Ōsada Hachiman Shrine (大貞八幡宮). The shrine features a sacred triangular pond, Misumi Pond (三角池, also historically written as 御澄池), representing the inner shrine, while the sacred hall is considered the outer shrine. This ancient shrine is said to be the progenitor of [[:en:Usa Jingū|Usa Shrine]], the primary shrine of all Hachiman shrines in Japan, with origins dating to the [[:en:Jōwa|Jōwa era]] (834–848). For this reason it is often called "Moto-Usa" == Enshrined Deities == * '''Main Hall''': ''' [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]] (Hachiman Ōkami) ''' {{ill|Hime-no-Kami|ja|比咩大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hime-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 比売神|1=Himegami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q22070227}} (The Three Go11esses of Munakata) ''' {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|息長帯比売命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Jingū|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神功皇后|1=Empress Jingū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q232803}} * '''Yasaka Shrine (Gion Shrine)''': ''' {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞嗚尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}} (Susanoo-no-Mikoto) * '''Wakamiya Shrine''': ''' Offspring of Emperor Ōjin Hime-no-Kami refers to the {{ill|Three Go11esses of Munakata|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=Three Munakata Go11esses|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Go11esses of Munakata|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=}}, represented by: * '''Tagorihime-no-Mikoto''' (沖津宮 at [[:en:Okinoshima (Fukuoka)|Okinoshima]]) * '''Tatsutsuhime-no-Mikoto''' (中津宮 at {{ill|Ōshima (Fukuoka)|ja|大島_(福岡県)|fa|اوشیما (استان فوکوئوکا)|ceb|Ō_Shima_(pulo_sa_Hapon,_Fukuoka-ken)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōshima (Fukuoka)|qq=}}) * '''Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto''' (辺津宮 at {{ill|Munakata Taisha|ja|宗像市田島|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata Taisha|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}). According to the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], Volume 1, Kami Age, Chapter 6, Variation 3, the Three Go11esses were said to have descended at Usa Island in Ashihara-no-Nakatsukuni, marking their connection to this sacred site. == Shrine Crest == The shrine uses the "Single Tomoe" crest (一つ巴). Unlike the "Triple Tomoe" of Usa Shrine, the Single Tomoe indicates Komo Shrine's status as a progenitor shrine. Some scholars suggest that the shrine originally enshrined one of the Three Go11esses, possibly Tatsutsuhime-no-Mikoto, rather than all three. == History == In [[:en:Yōrō|Yōrō]] 4 (720), during the [[:en:Hayato rebellion|Hayato Rebellion]], the forces led by [[:en:Ōtomo no Tabito|Ōtomo no Tabito]] and Usa's "Divine Army," under Karashima Haduome, used sacred reed mats (薦枕, Komomakura) from Misumi Pond as a divine artifact to quell the rebellion. The Komokari Festival commemorates this event and is still performed every six years as part of the {{ill|Usa Shrine Procession|ja|宇佐神宮行幸会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usa Shrine Procession|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} by the Karashima clan. Komo Shrine and Misumi Pond were designated as: * '''Ōita Prefectural Historic Site''' (March 30, 1976) * '''Ōita Prefectural Natural Monument''' for its aquatic and wetland plant communities. The Shinmon Gate, constructed by [[:en:Hosokawa Tadaoki|Hosokawa Tadaoki]] in [[:en:Yuanhe|Genna]] 8 (1622), is a [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]]. The shrine buildings date to the late Edo period. == Annual Rituals == * '''February 11''': Chinyoku Festival and Goshinkō-e (Demon Banishing Ceremony) * '''April 21''': festival * '''Third Saturday and Sunday in September''': Nakashū Festival (Sacred Procession) == Access == * From {{ill|Kokura Station|ja|小倉駅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokura Station|1=Kokura Station|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10957775}}: Take the [[:en:Sonic|Sonic (train)]], a limited express train on the [[:en:Nippō Main Line|Nippō Main Line]], to [[:en:Nakatsu Station|Nakatsu Station]] (approximately 30 minutes). A 15-minute drive from the station. * By Bus: From Nakatsu Station South Exit, take a {{ill|Daiwa Kotsu|ja|大交北部バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daiwa Kotsu|1=Daiko Hokubu Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432248}} bound for Tanaka or Shōnen Gakuin-Kansha-mae and alight at "Komo Shrine" (approximately 20 minutes). * By Car: Approximately 15 minutes from the Nakatsu IC on the {{ill|Nippō Expressway|ja|日豊自動車道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nippō Expressway|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. == References == * ''Photographic Collection: Komo Shrine, Shinmon Volume,'' Komo The shrine features a sacred triangular pond, Misumi Pond (三角池, also historically written as 御澄池), representing the inner shrine, while the sacred hall is considered the outer shrine. This ancient shrine is said to be the progenitor of [[:en:Usa Jingū|Usa Shrine]], the primary shrine of all Hachiman shrines in Japan, with origins dating to the [[:en:Jōwa|Jōwa era]] (834–848). For this reason it is often called "Moto-Usa" == Enshrined Deities == * '''Main Hall''': ''' [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]] (Hachiman Ōkami) ''' {{ill|Hime-no-Kami|ja|比咩大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hime-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 比売神|1=Himegami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q22070227}} (The Three Go11esses of Munakata) ''' {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|息長帯比売命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Jingū|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神功皇后|1=Empress Jingū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q232803}} * '''Yasaka Shrine (Gion Shrine)''': ''' {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞嗚尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}} (Susanoo-no-Mikoto) * '''Wakamiya Shrine''': ''' Offspring of Emperor Ōjin Hime-no-Kami refers to the {{ill|Three Go11esses of Munakata|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=Three Munakata Go11esses|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Go11esses of Munakata|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=}}, represented by: * '''Tagorihime-no-Mikoto''' (沖津宮 at [[:en:Okinoshima (Fukuoka)|Okinoshima]]) * '''Tatsutsuhime-no-Mikoto''' (中津宮 at {{ill|Ōshima (Fukuoka)|ja|大島_(福岡県)|fa|اوشیما (استان فوکوئوکا)|ceb|Ō_Shima_(pulo_sa_Hapon,_Fukuoka-ken)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōshima (Fukuoka)|qq=}}) * '''Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto''' (辺津宮 at {{ill|Munakata Taisha|ja|宗像市田島|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata Taisha|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}). According to the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], Volume 1, Kami Age, Chapter 6, Variation 3, the Three Go11esses were said to have descended at Usa Island in Ashihara-no-Nakatsukuni, marking their connection to this sacred site. == Shrine Crest == The shrine uses the "Single Tomoe" crest (一つ巴). Unlike the "Triple Tomoe" of Usa Shrine, the Single Tomoe indicates Komo Shrine's status as a progenitor shrine. Some scholars suggest that the shrine originally enshrined one of the Three Go11esses, possibly Tatsutsuhime-no-Mikoto, rather than all three. == History == In [[:en:Yōrō|Yōrō]] 4 (720), during the [[:en:Hayato rebellion|Hayato Rebellion]], the forces led by [[:en:Ōtomo no Tabito|Ōtomo no Tabito]] and Usa's "Divine Army," under Karashima Haduome, used sacred reed mats (薦枕, Komomakura) from Misumi Pond as a divine artifact to quell the rebellion. The Komokari Festival commemorates this event and is still performed every six years as part of the {{ill|Usa Shrine Procession|ja|宇佐神宮行幸会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usa Shrine Procession|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} by the Karashima clan. Komo Shrine and Misumi Pond were designated as: * '''Ōita Prefectural Historic Site''' (March 30, 1976) * '''Ōita Prefectural Natural Monument''' for its aquatic and wetland plant communities. The Shinmon Gate, constructed by [[:en:Hosokawa Tadaoki|Hosokawa Tadaoki]] in [[:en:Yuanhe|Genna]] 8 (1622), is a [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]]. The shrine buildings date to the late Edo period. == Annual Rituals == * '''February 11''': Chinyoku Festival and Goshinkō-e (Demon Banishing Ceremony) * '''April 21''': festival * '''Third Saturday and Sunday in September''': Nakashū Festival (Sacred Procession) == Access == * From {{ill|Kokura Station|ja|小倉駅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokura Station|1=Kokura Station|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10957775}}: Take the [[:en:Sonic|Sonic (train)]], a limited express train on the [[:en:Nippō Main Line|Nippō Main Line]], to [[:en:Nakatsu Station|Nakatsu Station]] (approximately 30 minutes). A 15-minute drive from the station. * By Bus: From Nakatsu Station South Exit, take a {{ill|Daiwa Kotsu|ja|大交北部バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daiwa Kotsu|1=Daiko Hokubu Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432248}} bound for Tanaka or Shōnen Gakuin-Kansha-mae and alight at "Komo Shrine" (approximately 20 minutes). * By Car: Approximately 15 minutes from the Nakatsu IC on the {{ill|Nippō Expressway|ja|日豊自動車道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nippō Expressway|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. == References == * ''Photographic Collection: Komo Shrine, Shinmon Volume,'' Komo Culture Research Institute, 1999 * Kenichi Tanigawa (Ed.), ''Japanese Gods and Sacred Sites, Volume 1: Kyūshū,'' Hakusuisha, 1984, ISBN 4-560-02211-9 * ''Cultural Properties of Ōita Prefecture,'' Ōita Prefectural Board of Education, 1991 == See Also == * [[:en:Hata clan|Hata clan]] * {{ill|Daigo Hisa|ja|大悟法久志|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daigo Hisa|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://www.komojinja.jp/ Official Website of Komo Shrine] {{Shinto navbox}} {{translated page|ja|薦神社|version=101324642|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Shrines dedicated to Empress Jingū]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:17th-century architecture]] [[Category:Nakatsu, Ōita]] }} [[ja:薦神社]] [[ja:薦神社]] [[ja:薦神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11622260]] Wikidata name: Komo-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:37, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11622260]] Wikidata name: Komo-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:16, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Komo Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Komo.jpg | A11ress = [[:en:Ōita Prefecture|Ōita Prefecture]], [[:en:Nakatsu, Ōita|Nakatsu, Ōita]], Ōaza Osada 209 | coordinates = {{Coord|33.567278|N|131.218139|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | Deities = [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]]
{{ill|Hime-no-Kami|ja|比売大神|lt=Hime-no-Ōkami|1=Himegami|3=比売神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hime-no-Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Himegami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 比売神}}
{{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|息長帯比売命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Jingū|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神功皇后|1=Empress Jingū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q232803}} | Status = Former [[Prefectural Shrine]] (県社) | Established = [[Shōwa era (Japan)|Shōwa era]] (9th century) | Main Hall = [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] | Alternate name = Ōsada Hachiman Shrine (大貞八幡宮) | Festivals = April 21 (festival) | Map = {{location map|Japan Oita|label=Komo Shrine|lat_deg=33|lat_min=34|lat_sec=2.2|lon_deg=131|lon_min=13|lon_sec=5.1|position=top|width=210|float=center|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=210|coord={{coord|33|34|2.2|N|131|13|5.1|E}}}} | prefecture= {{ill|Nakatsu|WD=Q861221}} | architecture_style= {{ill|nagare-zukuri|WD=Q210684}} }} '''Komo Shrine''' (薦神社) is a [[:en:Hachiman shrine|Hachiman Shrine]] located in [[:en:Nakatsu, Ōita|Nakatsu, Ōita]], [[:en:Ōita Prefecture|Ōita Prefecture]]. It is also referred to as the Ōsada Hachiman Shrine (大貞八幡宮). The shrine features a sacred triangular pond, Misumi Pond (三角池, also historically written as 御澄池), representing the inner shrine, while the sacred hall is considered the outer shrine. This ancient shrine is said to be the progenitor of [[:en:Usa Jingū|Usa Shrine]], the primary shrine of all Hachiman shrines in Japan, with origins dating to the [[:en:Jōwa|Jōwa era]] (834–848). For this reason it is often called "Moto-Usa" == Enshrined Deities == * '''Main Hall''': ''' [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]] (Hachiman Ōkami) ''' {{ill|Hime-no-Kami|ja|比咩大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hime-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 比売神|1=Himegami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q22070227}} (The Three Go11esses of Munakata) ''' {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|息長帯比売命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Jingū|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神功皇后|1=Empress Jingū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q232803}} * '''Yasaka Shrine (Gion Shrine)''': ''' {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞嗚尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}} (Susanoo-no-Mikoto) * '''Wakamiya Shrine''': ''' Offspring of Emperor Ōjin Hime-no-Kami refers to the {{ill|Three Go11esses of Munakata|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=Three Munakata Go11esses|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Go11esses of Munakata|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=}}, represented by: * '''Tagorihime-no-Mikoto''' (沖津宮 at [[:en:Okinoshima (Fukuoka)|Okinoshima]]) * '''Tatsutsuhime-no-Mikoto''' (中津宮 at {{ill|Ōshima (Fukuoka)|ja|大島_(福岡県)|fa|اوشیما (استان فوکوئوکا)|ceb|Ō_Shima_(pulo_sa_Hapon,_Fukuoka-ken)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōshima (Fukuoka)|qq=}}) * '''Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto''' (辺津宮 at {{ill|Munakata Taisha|ja|宗像市田島|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata Taisha|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}). According to the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], Volume 1, Kami Age, Chapter 6, Variation 3, the Three Go11esses were said to have descended at Usa Island in Ashihara-no-Nakatsukuni, marking their connection to this sacred site. == Shrine Crest == The shrine uses the "Single Tomoe" crest (一つ巴). Unlike the "Triple Tomoe" of Usa Shrine, the Single Tomoe indicates Komo Shrine's status as a progenitor shrine. Some scholars suggest that the shrine originally enshrined one of the Three Go11esses, possibly Tatsutsuhime-no-Mikoto, rather than all three. == History == In [[:en:Yōrō|Yōrō]] 4 (720), during the [[:en:Hayato rebellion|Hayato Rebellion]], the forces led by [[:en:Ōtomo no Tabito|Ōtomo no Tabito]] and Usa's "Divine Army," under Karashima Haduome, used sacred reed mats (薦枕, Komomakura) from Misumi Pond as a divine artifact to quell the rebellion. The Komokari Festival commemorates this event and is still performed every six years as part of the {{ill|Usa Shrine Procession|ja|宇佐神宮行幸会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usa Shrine Procession|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} by the Karashima clan. Komo Shrine and Misumi Pond were designated as: * '''Ōita Prefectural Historic Site''' (March 30, 1976) * '''Ōita Prefectural Natural Monument''' for its aquatic and wetland plant communities. The Shinmon Gate, constructed by [[:en:Hosokawa Tadaoki|Hosokawa Tadaoki]] in [[:en:Yuanhe|Genna]] 8 (1622), is a [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]]. The shrine buildings date to the late Edo period. == Annual Rituals == * '''February 11''': Chinyoku Festival and Goshinkō-e (Demon Banishing Ceremony) * '''April 21''': festival * '''Third Saturday and Sunday in September''': Nakashū Festival (Sacred Procession) == Access == * From {{ill|Kokura Station|ja|小倉駅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokura Station|1=Kokura Station|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10957775}}: Take the [[:en:Sonic|Sonic (train)]], a limited express train on the [[:en:Nippō Main Line|Nippō Main Line]], to [[:en:Nakatsu Station|Nakatsu Station]] (approximately 30 minutes). A 15-minute drive from the station. * By Bus: From Nakatsu Station South Exit, take a {{ill|Daiwa Kotsu|ja|大交北部バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daiwa Kotsu|1=Daiko Hokubu Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432248}} bound for Tanaka or Shōnen Gakuin-Kansha-mae and alight at "Komo Shrine" (approximately 20 minutes). * By Car: Approximately 15 minutes from the Nakatsu IC on the {{ill|Nippō Expressway|ja|日豊自動車道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nippō Expressway|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. == References == * ''Photographic Collection: Komo Shrine, Shinmon Volume,'' Komo Culture Research Institute, 1999 * Kenichi Tanigawa (Ed.), ''Japanese Gods and Sacred Sites, Volume 1: Kyūshū,'' Hakusuisha, 1984, ISBN 4-560-02211-9 * ''Cultural Properties of Ōita Prefecture,'' Ōita Prefectural Board of Education, 1991 == See Also == * [[:en:Hata clan|Hata clan]] * {{ill|Daigo Hisa|ja|大悟法久志|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daigo Hisa|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://www.komojinja.jp/ Official Website of Komo Shrine] {{Shinto navbox}} {{translated page|ja|薦神社|version=101324642|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Shrines dedicated to Empress Jingū]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:17th-century architecture]] [[Category:Nakatsu, Ōita]] }} historic sites of Oita Prefecture]] [[Category:Natural Monuments Designated in Oita Prefecture]] [[Category:Cultural heritage monuments in Japan]] {{wikidata link|Q11622260}} [[Category:Wikidata has The Shinmon Gate, constructed by [[:en:Hosokawa Tadaoki|Hosokawa Tadaoki]] in [[:en:Yuanhe|Genna]] 8 (1622), is a [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]]. The shrine buildings date to the late Edo period. == Annual Rituals == * '''February 11''': Chinyoku Festival and Goshinkō-e (Demon Banishing Ceremony) * '''April 21''': festival * '''Third Saturday and Sunday in September''': Nakashū Festival (Sacred Procession) == Access == * From {{ill|Kokura Station|ja|小倉駅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokura Station|1=Kokura Station|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10957775}}: Take the [[:en:Sonic|Sonic (train)]], a limited express train on the [[:en:Nippō Main Line|Nippō Main Line]], to [[:en:Nakatsu Station|Nakatsu Station]] (approximately 30 minutes). A 15-minute drive from the station. * By Bus: From Nakatsu Station South Exit, take a {{ill|Daiwa Kotsu|ja|大交北部バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daiwa Kotsu|1=Daiko Hokubu Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432248}} bound for Tanaka or Shōnen Gakuin-Kansha-mae and alight at "Komo Shrine" (approximately 20 minutes). * By Car: Approximately 15 minutes from the Nakatsu IC on the {{ill|Nippō Expressway|ja|日豊自動車道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nippō Expressway|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. == References == * ''Photographic Collection: Komo Shrine, Shinmon Volume,'' Komo Culture Research Institute, 1999 * Kenichi Tanigawa (Ed.), ''Japanese Gods and Sacred Sites, Volume 1: Kyūshū,'' Hakusuisha, 1984, ISBN 4-560-02211-9 * ''Cultural Properties of Ōita Prefecture,'' Ōita Prefectural Board of Education, 1991 == See Also == * [[:en:Hata clan|Hata clan]] * {{ill|Daigo Hisa|ja|大悟法久志|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daigo Hisa|qq=}} == External Links == *
